title: Fractional CFO / Controller — Real Estate & Beauty Portfolio
description:
Marshell Group Inc. is seeking an experienced Fractional CFO / Controller to establish and manage the financial infrastructure for our holding company and affiliated entities. This is a hands\-on, operational finance role — not advisory only.

You will build systems, close books, manage compliance, and provide strategic financial guidance to the CEO directly.

Key Details:

* Location: Remote with periodic on\-site presence (Los Angeles, CA)
* Compensation: TBD — commensurate with experience
* Type: Contract / Fractional \| 15–20 hours/month
* 6\-month initial engagement with mutual renewal option
* Background check required

Scope of Services:

* Establish accounting systems and manage monthly financial close for all entities
* Payroll processing oversight for W\-2 employees and independent contractors
* Facilitate annual tax preparation and filing (federal and state) for all MG entities \+ CEO personal returns
* Provide financial analysis, investor reporting, and capital deployment tracking
* Develop budgets, forecasts, and financial models for existing and new locations
* Support SBA lending relationships and bank covenant compliance

Requirements:

* CPA license (active, in good standing) or equivalent professional certification
* 7\+ years in financial management, controllership, or CFO roles
* Multi\-entity corporate structures and consolidated financial reporting experience
* Strong knowledge of GAAP, tax compliance, and payroll regulations
* Experience with real estate, property management, or construction finance
* Proficiency with QuickBooks, Xero, or similar accounting platforms

How to Apply:

Email your resume, a brief engagement proposal, and references to careers@marshellgroup.com

Subject Line: "Fractional CFO Application \- \[Your Name]"

Or apply directly at marshellgroup.com/careers

Marshell Group Inc. is an equal opportunity employer.**Job Overview**  
We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Fractional CFO / Controller to oversee the financial operations of a diverse portfolio spanning real estate and beauty industries. This role offers an exciting opportunity to lead financial strategy, ensure compliance with accounting standards, and drive growth through insightful analysis and effective risk management. As a key member of the leadership team, you will provide expert financial guidance, support strategic planning, and foster sustainable business development across multiple entities. Your expertise will empower our organization to optimize revenue management, streamline fiscal processes, and uphold the highest standards of financial integrity.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead comprehensive financial planning, forecasting, and budgeting processes to support strategic objectives.
* Oversee corporate accounting functions, ensuring adherence to GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), and other relevant regulations.
* Manage risk analysis and develop mitigation strategies, including compliance with SOX (Sarbanes\-Oxley Act) controls and COSO (Committee of Sponsoring Organizations) frameworks.
* Supervise treasury management activities such as cash flow optimization, expense control, and supply chain finance.
* Conduct detailed financial analysis for profit \& loss statements, revenue cycle management, and cost control initiatives to maximize profitability.
* Support mergers \& acquisitions by performing due diligence, valuation modeling, and integration planning.
* Prepare clear, comprehensive financial reports for senior leadership and external stakeholders; interpret complex financial data for strategic decision\-making.
* Ensure regulatory reporting compliance across all entities while maintaining rigorous internal controls.
* Lead efforts in business development by analyzing market trends, conducting market analysis, and identifying growth opportunities within the portfolio.
* Supervise accounting teams responsible for payroll management, cost accounting, tax accounting, and technical accounting functions such as ASC 606 (Revenue Recognition) and IFRS standards.

**Qualifications**

* Proven experience in senior financial management roles with a focus on real estate or beauty industries preferred.
* Extensive knowledge of corporate finance principles including budgeting, forecasting, risk management, and fiscal management.
* Strong understanding of financial software systems and tools used for financial modeling, analysis, and reporting.
* Demonstrated expertise in SOX compliance, GAAP/IFRS standards, governmental accounting, and regulatory reporting requirements.
* Skilled in negotiation, business development strategies, and strategic planning with a focus on revenue growth.
* Excellent analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex financial concepts and produce actionable insights.
* Supervisory experience managing finance teams across multiple functions such as payroll management, cost control, and supply chain finance.
* Effective communicator with strong leadership qualities capable of collaborating with senior leadership and guiding cross\-functional teams.
* Technical proficiency in financial concepts like profit \& loss analysis, expense management, risk assessment, and cost control measures. Join us to lead innovative financial strategies that empower our diverse portfolio’s growth while maintaining rigorous standards of compliance and operational excellence!

Pay: $2,500\.00 \- $5,000\.00 per month

Work Location: Remote

title: Construction Accounting Manager
description:
Job Summary:

This is a hands\-on, full\-charge accounting role responsible for executing, managing, and reviewing all accounting functions within a one\-person department. The Accounting Manager will perform daily accounting tasks while also ensuring accuracy, maintaining internal controls, and providing financial insight to leadership. This role requires ownership of Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, payroll support, compliance, reconciliations, and financial reporting, along with continuous process improvement and problem\-solving.

This role is expected to bring structure, consistency, and continuous improvement to the company’s accounting processes. The Accounting Manager must operate independently, take full ownership of financial accuracy and timelines, and serve as a reliable financial resource for leadership.

Success in This Role Looks Like:

\- Monthly financials are accurate and delivered by agreed\-upon deadlines

\- Accounts Payable and Accounts Receivable are current and well\-managed

\- Job costing data is accurate and supports operational decision\-making

\- Cash flow is clearly understood and proactively monitored

\- Accounting systems (QuickBooks and BuildOps) are aligned and reconciled

\- Financial records are organized, accurate, and audit\-ready

\- Leadership can rely on timely, clear financial reporting and insight

Key Responsibilities:

Accounts Payable (AP):

\- Perform and manage all Accounts Payable processes, including invoice intake, warehouse coordination for material receipt verification, coding, entry into QuickBooks and BuildOps, and payment tracking

Accounts Receivable (AR):

\- Perform and manage all Accounts Receivable functions, including invoicing, payment processing, deposits, and collections

\- Process and apply customer payments through BuildOps, PayTrace, and third\-party processors, ensuring accurate application and documentation

Job Costing \& Project Accounting:

\- Maintain and review job costing data between BuildOps and QuickBooks to ensure accuracy

\- Assist in tracking project profitability and cost allocation

\- Support Work\-in\-Progress (WIP) reporting and revenue recognition as applicable

General Accounting:

\- Perform bank reconciliations, credit card reconciliations, and maintain the general ledger

\- Monitor cash flow and account balances, identifying issues or discrepancies

Financial Reporting \& Close:

\- Prepare, review, and analyze monthly financial statements and reports

\- Own and execute the monthly close process, including journal entries, accruals, and reconciliations

Payroll \& Expenses:

\- Support payroll processing, journal entries, and employee reimbursements

\- Coordinate payroll with third\-party provider, including review, journal entries, and employee reimbursements

\- Manage employee expense submissions, ensuring proper documentation, naming conventions, and coding

Compliance:

\- Handle sales tax filings, 1099s, and assist with quarterly and year\-end tax requirements

\- Maintain business license compliance across jurisdictions

External Coordination:

\- Coordinate with external CPA and fractional CFO for reporting and financial review

\- Prepare documentation and support for external audits, financial reviews, or lender requests

Records Management:

\- Maintain accurate vendor and customer records, including tax codes, terms, and compliance documentation

\- Maintain organized, audit\-ready financial records and documentation

\- Serve as the primary point of contact for all accounting\-related questions and issue resolution

Review \& Controls:

\- Review all accounting entries for accuracy, completeness, and proper coding

\- Ensure proper separation of duties where possible and identify risk areas

\- Maintain internal controls over payments, deposits, and financial reporting

\- Verify invoice processing workflows, including warehouse confirmation for materials

\- Ensure payments are properly applied and reconciled across systems

\- Identify and recommend improvements to internal controls in a limited\-segregation environment

\- Review aging reports, cash balances, and outstanding items regularly

\- Identify discrepancies and resolve issues proactively

Process Improvement \& Systems:

\- Identify inefficiencies and implement process improvements

\- Maintain and improve accounting procedures and workflows

\- Ensure proper use of QuickBooks, BuildOps, PayTrace, and Treasury Navigator

\- Assist in automation and system improvements where possible

\- Document and maintain accounting SOPs

\- Standardize and enforce accounting processes across departments

Financial Oversight \& Planning:

\- Assist with budgeting and financial planning

\- Monitor performance against budget and identify variances

\- Provide financial insights and recommendations to leadership

\- Support cash flow planning and forecasting

\- Assist with long\-term financial organization and planning

Systems \& Tools:

\- QuickBooks Online

\- BuildOps

\- PayTrace (or equivalent)

\- SouthState Treasury Navigator

\- Microsoft Excel

Qualifications:

\- Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

\- 5\+ years of hands\-on accounting experience in a full\-charge or similar role

\- Experience in construction, service, or project\-based accounting

\- Strong knowledge of GAAP and accounting principles

\- Proficiency in QuickBooks and Excel

\- Experience independently managing AP, AR, and reconciliations

\- Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

\- Ability to manage a full accounting function independently

\- Strong problem\-solving and communication skills

\- Ability to operate independently and manage a full accounting function with minimal supervision

\- High level of accountability for accuracy, organization, and deadlines

Preferred:

\- Experience with job costing and project\-based financial tracking

\- Familiarity with BuildOps or similar operational/accounting systems

\- Experience working with external CPAs or fractional CFOs

Compensation:

Salary: Up to $80,000 per year, based on experience

Pay: $70,000\.00 \- $80,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) 3% Match
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Fractional CFO/Controller (Small Business) Lansing Area
description:

title: Controller
description:
**Controller**  
**Location:** Las Colinas (Irving, TX) – Hybrid  
**Employment Type:** Full\-Time  
**Salary Range:** $90,000 – $115,000

**About Meredith Vantage**

Meredith Vantage is a growing advisory and accounting firm based in Las Colinas. We work with entrepreneurs, privately held companies, and high\-net\-worth families. Our focus is simple: do good work, give clients real insight, and build a team that actually enjoys coming to work.

We are adding one, possibly two Controllers to the team. One role will support our fractional CFO practice, working directly with clients. The other will focus internally on accounting and bookkeeping operations.

**The Role**

This is a hands\-on accounting role for someone who knows how to own the numbers and keep things clean. You will be trusted to manage financials, support leadership, and bring structure where it’s needed.

Depending on the seat, you will either:

* Work directly with clients alongside our fractional CFO team, or
* Own internal accounting and bookkeeping for the firm

**What You’ll Do**

* Manage monthly close and maintain accurate financial reporting
* Prepare and review financial statements
* Handle general ledger, reconciliations, and journal entries
* Support budgeting, forecasting, and cash flow tracking
* Assist with process improvements and reporting visibility
* Work closely with leadership to ensure financial clarity
* For client\-facing role: act as a key financial contact for multiple clients

**What We’re Looking For**

* Public accounting background required
* Strong understanding of GAAP and financial reporting
* Experience owning the close process
* Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, evolving environment
* Able to operate independently without constant oversight
* Strong attention to detail without overcomplicating things
* Experience with QuickBooks or similar systems preferred

**Why Meredith Vantage**

* Direct access to leadership and real decision\-making
* Variety of work across different industries and clients
* Clear opportunity to grow into higher\-level roles
* No unnecessary layers or red tape

**Additional Notes**

We are open to hiring two strong candidates depending on fit:

* One focused on client\-facing, fractional CFO support
* One focused on internal accounting and bookkeeping

If you’re someone who likes clean financials, straightforward communication, and being trusted to do your job, you’ll fit in well here.

Pay: $90,000\.00 \- $115,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Irving, TX 75039

title: Chief Financial Officer
description:
**Location:** Ontario, CA (On\-site)  
**Role:** CFO

Reports to: CEO/Owner \& President

**Role Overview**

The **Chief Financial Officer (CFO)** provides both **strategic and hands\-on financial leadership** across a growing, multi\-entity organization with 80\-100 employees. This role is responsible for financial strategy, reporting integrity, internal controls, audit readiness, and transaction execution.

The CFO partners closely with the **CEO and President** to align financial performance with operational execution and long\-term growth objectives. This is an **on\-site, fractional consulting role** requiring a strong operator mindset and the ability to move seamlessly between strategy and execution.

**Core Responsibilities**

* Lead enterprise\-wide financial strategy, planning, budgeting, and forecasting
* Oversee accounting and financial reporting across multiple legal entities
* Ensure accurate consolidations, intercompany accounting, and financial statement integrity
* Design, implement, and enforce strong internal controls and proper segregation of duties
* Lead transaction activity, including QOE/QoE, sell\-side readiness, and lender\-facing diligence
* Manage banking relationships, cash flow, and capital strategy
* Provide hands\-on leadership to the accounting and finance team, including restructuring, mentoring, and performance improvement as needed
* Support audit readiness and external stakeholder interactions
* Proven experience in senior leadership roles within finance or accounting departments; prior CFO experience preferred.
* Extensive knowledge of GAAP, IFRS, IAS standards, ASC 606, COSO frameworks, and regulatory reporting requirements.
* Strong background in corporate finance including mergers \& acquisitions, revenue cycle management, budgeting, forecasting, and profit \& loss analysis.
* Demonstrated expertise in financial software systems and advanced financial modeling skills.
* Excellent understanding of governmental accounting practices as well as non\-profit accounting if applicable.
* Skilled in risk analysis, risk management strategies, cost control measures, and supply chain finance.
* Exceptional leadership abilities with experience supervising teams across various finance functions such as payroll management and expense control.
* Strong analytical skills combined with strategic thinking capabilities; adept at business development initiatives.
* Effective negotiation skills with a track record of fostering stakeholder relationships at senior levels.
* Relevant educational background in finance or accounting; professional certifications such as CPA are required. This role offers an opportunity for a dynamic leader to influence the future direction of our organization through expert financial stewardship while working within a collaborative executive team committed to excellence in governance and operational success.

**Required Experience \& Qualifications**

* 10\+ years of progressive finance and accounting leadership experience
* Strong background in multi\-entity accounting environments
* Proven transaction and deal experience (QOE/QoE, sell\-side, lender\-facing)
* Demonstrated ability to build, manage, and restructure accounting teams
* Hands\-on leadership style with a strong internal controls mindset
* Bachelor’s degree in Finance or Accounting required
* CPA required

Pay: $100,000\.00 \- $250,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Education:

* Bachelor's (Required)

Experience:

* Accounting: 10 years (Required)

License/Certification:

* and active CPA License (Required)

Ability to Commute:

* Ontario, CA 91762 (Required)

Work Location: In person

title: Strategic CFO (Contract, GovCon, Hybrid) – Finance as a Service (FaaS)
description:
**Strategic CFO – Finance as a Service (FaaS) \| GovConGrowth Companies**




**About ROSE**


At ROSE Financial Solutions, we believe finance should not be a reporting function\-it should be a **strategic engine for growth**.




We are on a mission to **automate 85% of finance and accounting by 2030**, transforming the back office into a proactive, decision\-ready function that empowers leaders to scale with confidence.




**The Opportunity**


We are seeking experienced CFOs who want to move beyond traditional roles and become **strategic leaders in a next\-generation finance model**.




This is not a typical fractional CFO role.




You will:

* Help clients become **decision\-ready, audit\-ready, and scale\-ready**
* Build **financial infrastructure**, not just reports
* Leverage **AI and technology (Easby)** to transform finance functions
* Serve as a **trusted advisor to CEOs and executive teams**




**What You'll Do**

* Act as a **strategic CFO partner** to multiple growth\-stage organizations
* Lead **financial strategy, forecasting, and performance optimization**
* Design and implement **scalable financial infrastructure**
* Ensure **DCAA, FAR, and regulatory compliance (GovCon focus)**
* Drive **data\-driven decision\-making and KPI visibility**
* Integrate **technology, automation, and AI into finance workflows**
* Support **M\&A, capital raises, and strategic growth initiatives**
* Mentor controllers, accounting teams, and client stakeholders




**What Makes This Role Different**


At ROSE, you won't just:

* Close books
* Review reports
* Fix problems




You will:

* Build systems that eliminate those problems permanently
* Help clients **scale from $2M**  **$50M\+ with confidence**
* Be part of the transformation of the finance industry




**Who You Are**

* Former CFO or senior finance leader (**10–20\+ years experience**)
* Strong background in **GovCon, compliance, or regulated industries (preferred)**
* Deep expertise in **financial modeling, FP\&A, and strategic finance**
* Passionate about **technology, automation, and innovation in finance**
* Able to communicate with and influence **CEOs and executive teams**
* Excited by **building\-not just advising**




**Why ROSE**

* Work with **high\-growth companies and mission\-driven leaders**
* Be part of a firm redefining finance through **FaaS \+ AI**
* Collaborate with a **team of experts across accounting, tax, and compliance**
* Flexible structure with **meaningful impact on every client**




**Call to Action**

* If you want to move from reactive finance to building the future of finance\-apply now.

title: Digital Marketing Strategist
description:
**Who We Are**

Disruptors Media is an AI\-powered marketing agency based in North Salt Lake, Utah. We serve as a fractional CMO and Chief AI Officer for businesses across multiple industries, specializing in AI automation, lead generation, content creation, and marketing systems that drive real revenue.

Our philosophy is simple. AI should eliminate repetitive work so marketers can focus on strategy, creativity, and impact. We don’t just advise. We build, execute, and optimize systems that transform how our clients grow.

We are a small, high\-performing team where every role directly impacts outcomes. There is no bureaucracy and no room to coast. If you want to do meaningful work and see the results of what you build, you will fit in well here.

**The Role**

We are looking for a Digital Marketing Specialist who will execute, optimize, and scale marketing campaigns across multiple client accounts. This is a hands\-on role focused on performance, not theory.

You will be responsible for building campaigns, managing content and automation systems, analyzing performance, and continuously improving results. This is not a passive execution role. You are expected to think critically, spot opportunities, and take ownership of outcomes.

The right person is both creative and analytical. Someone who can build campaigns, interpret data, and make smart decisions quickly.

**What You Will Do**

\- Campaign Execution and Optimization

\- Build and manage marketing campaigns across channels including paid ads, email, SMS, and organic content  
\- Launch and optimize funnels designed to generate leads and drive conversions  
Continuously test messaging, creatives, and targeting to improve performance  
Monitor campaign performance daily and make data\-driven adjustments

**Content and Automation**

\- Create and manage content across platforms including social media, email, and landing pages  
\- Build and maintain marketing automation workflows using tools like GoHighLevel or similar platforms  
\- Ensure campaigns and automations are functioning properly and delivering a strong user experience  
\- Collaborate with internal teams on video, creative, and copy assets

**Performance Tracking and Reporting**

\- Track key metrics across all campaigns including cost per lead, conversion rates, and ROI  
\- Pull and analyze data to identify trends, issues, and opportunities  
\- Prepare performance summaries and insights that inform future strategy  
\- Maintain accurate data inside CRM and marketing platforms

**Support Client Growth**

\- Work closely with internal strategists to execute marketing plans  
\- Identify opportunities to improve campaign performance and expand results  
\- Contribute ideas that improve client outcomes and internal processes  
\- Take ownership of results and continuously push for better performance

**Who You Are**

The Non\-Negotiables

\- You live in or are willing to relocate to the greater Salt Lake area. This is an in\-person role  
\- You have 2\+ years of experience in digital marketing, campaign management, or a related role  
\- You are both creative and analytical. You can build campaigns and understand the numbers behind them  
\- You are highly organized and can manage multiple campaigns and deadlines simultaneously  
\- You take ownership of your work and consistently follow through  
\- You are comfortable learning and using AI tools as part of your workflow

**What Sets You Apart**

\- Experience running paid ads on platforms like Meta or Google  
\- Familiarity with CRM and automation tools such as GoHighLevel, HubSpot, or Salesforce  
\- Experience with funnel building, landing pages, and conversion optimization  
\- Ability to interpret data and turn insights into action  
\- Agency or fast\-paced environment experience

**What a Typical Week Looks Like**

Monday  
Team meeting and campaign planning. Review performance metrics and identify priorities. Launch or adjust campaigns.

Tuesday  
Build and optimize campaigns. Create content and update automation workflows. Monitor performance and make adjustments.

Wednesday  
Deep work. Analyze campaign data, test new ideas, and build new funnels or automations.

Thursday  
Campaign optimization and performance tracking. Collaborate with internal teams on creative and messaging.

Friday  
Reporting, cleanup, and planning. Review results, document insights, and prepare for the following week.

**What We Offer**

Competitive Pay with performance\-based growth

Growth Potential in a fast\-growing agency where strong performers advance quickly

AI Skill Development using tools that are shaping the future of marketing

Real Impact with direct visibility into results and outcomes

Training and Systems with proven frameworks and processes

Team Culture built around high standards, accountability, and support

**This Role Is Not for You If**

You prefer passive work or need constant direction  
You are not comfortable working with data and performance metrics  
You avoid accountability for results  
You are not interested in learning AI tools and modern marketing systems  
You want a low\-pressure, predictable role

**How to Apply**

Submit your application through Indeed with your resume

In your application, answer this question: Tell us about a marketing campaign you worked on. What was your role, what did you do, and what were the results?

If we see a strong fit, we will reach out to schedule a conversation

We move quickly

Disruptors Media is an equal opportunity employer and evaluates candidates based on ability, performance, and alignment with our mission

Pay: $3,000\.00 \- $5,000\.00 per month

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

Work Location: In person

title: Fractional CMO
description:
Fractional CMO  


VP Marketing \| Chief Marketing Officer \| Marketing Consultant  


Develop comprehensive B2B marketing strategies, lead client engagements, and architect multi\-year marketing programs for an established agency (since 2009\) serving professional services firms across multiple verticals. Own comprehensive marketing strategies and multi\-year marketing action plans for multiple concurrent clients. This is a senior strategic role \- you develop the strategies and plans that execution teams implement.  


This is a primarily remote position with occasional in\-person collaboration in the Tampa Bay area (quarterly or as needed). Tampa Bay area candidates strongly preferred; open to exceptional candidates in other US states.  


Key Skills: B2B Marketing Strategy \| Strategic Planning \| Brand Strategy \| Go\-to\-Market Strategy \| Demand Generation \| ABM \| Content Strategy \| Customer Journey Mapping \| Sales Enablement \| MarTech Architecture \| AI Marketing Strategy \| Client Engagement  


This Role Is Perfect For Someone Who:  


Has 20\+ years developing comprehensive B2B marketing strategies across multiple industries  
Is building or growing a fractional practice and wants a steady, high\-quality client base through an agency partnership  
Thinks in frameworks; you build structured, phased marketing roadmaps, not ad hoc recommendations  
Is equally comfortable presenting to a CEO and briefing an execution team on campaign specifications  
Wants strategic ownership across multiple client engagements without managing a team  
Can teach and document your strategic thinking, not just produce deliverables  
Values working within a proven methodology while bringing your own strategic depth to it  


This Role Is NOT a Fit If:  


Your primary strength is tactical execution (campaign building, platform configuration, ads management)  
You prefer a single deep client engagement over multiple concurrent accounts  
You want a full\-time position with benefits (this is a contractor role with variable hours)  
You need to be told what to do; this role requires independent strategic judgment  
You view budget guardrails, scope boundaries, and approval processes as unnecessary constraints on your autonomy  
Your B2B experience is limited or primarily B2C  


What We Offer  


Strategic ownership \- you own client marketing strategy from assessment through multi\-year planning, with experienced leadership providing business context and quality standards  
Established methodology \- our Marketing Direction Strategic Blueprint® methodology and Detailed Marketing Action Plan (DMAP) framework give you proven structures to work within, not a blank slate  
Senior collaboration \- work alongside experienced strategists and directly with agency leadership; implementation teams are senior\-level professionals, not junior resources  
Variety with depth \- professional services clients across multiple verticals, each with unique market dynamics  
Flexibility \- variable hours based on client demand and your availability; fully remote; no rigid schedule  


Responsibilities  


Strategic Planning \& Program Development  


Develop comprehensive marketing strategies and multi\-year marketing action plans for B2B professional services clients  
Translate client business objectives into structured marketing programs with clear deliverables, timelines, and budget allocations  
Develop brand strategy (positioning, messaging), go\-to\-market strategy, demand generation strategy, ABM strategy, and content strategy  
Map customer journeys and develop sales enablement frameworks aligned with client growth objectives  
Architect integrated marketing campaigns across digital, content, and traditional channels  
Present strategies to client leadership with confidence and clarity (PowerPoint, Excel proficiency required)  


Client Engagement  


Lead strategic discussions with client leadership to understand business goals, challenges, and market position  
Serve as senior strategic point of contact for assigned client engagements  
Deliver strategic work within established scope, timeline, and budget parameters, flagging concerns proactively  


MarTech Strategy  


Evaluate and recommend marketing technology solutions aligned with client objectives  
Architect CRM, automation, and analytics strategies at a strategic level (platform selection, data architecture, integration approach)  
Develop lead scoring frameworks, nurture strategy, and conversion optimization approaches  
Stay current on AI marketing applications and evaluate opportunities to integrate AI\-powered tools into client marketing programs  


Knowledge Transfer \& Documentation  


Document strategic frameworks and decision rationale clearly enough for execution teams to implement without ambiguity  
Brief execution teams on strategic intent behind tactical specifications; you provide strategic direction, they execute under agency operations leadership  
Contribute to agency intellectual property by refining methodologies and developing repeatable frameworks  


Qualifications  


Required:  


20\+ years of progressive B2B marketing experience \- with a clear arc from execution to strategic leadership  
Strategic planning expertise \- proven experience developing comprehensive, multi\-year marketing strategies (not just campaign plans)  
Multi\-industry B2B experience \- demonstrated work across multiple professional services verticals (e.g., IT/MSP, accounting, legal, insurance, consulting, architecture/engineering)  
Agency, consulting, or fractional background \- at least one year of experience managing strategic work across multiple concurrent client engagements  
Client\-facing confidence \- experience presenting to C\-suite and leading strategic conversations with business owners  
Budget and scope discipline \- proven ability to deliver within budget, manage scope, and communicate proactively when adjustments are needed  
Exceptional communication skills \- written and verbal; compelling strategic presentations and clear articulation of complex ideas  
Bachelor's degree in business administration, marketing, or related field  


Preferred:  


Fortune 500 marketing experience (strategy, planning, or senior marketing leadership)  
MBA or advanced degree  
MarTech architecture experience, including platform selection, CRM strategy, automation design  
Experience with HubSpot, Salesforce, GoHighLevel, or similar at a strategic/architectural level  
Content strategy and thought leadership program development  
Former VP Marketing, CMO, or senior agency strategist background  
Advanced AI prompt engineering for strategic planning, research, and analytical applications (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini)  


About Marketing Direction  


Marketing Direction is a strategic B2B marketing agency established in 2009, specializing in comprehensive marketing solutions for professional services firms. We serve clients across MSP/IT services, accounting, insurance, HR consulting, legal services, and architecture/engineering.  


We're known for our Marketing Direction Strategic Blueprint® methodology and Detailed Marketing Action Plans (DMAPs) that provide clients with clear, transparent roadmaps from strategy through execution.  


Company Values: Action\-Oriented, Strategic, Passionate, Innovative, Results\-Driven, Expertise  


Evaluation Process  


We move efficiently with qualified candidates. Application review, screening conversation, strategic assessment with practical component, final conversation and decision.  


How to Apply  


Submit your application through our online application system.  


Required with your application:  


Resume highlighting strategic marketing experience and career progression  
Desired hourly rate (applications without rate expectations will not be considered)  
Availability (estimated hours per week and any constraints)  
Work samples demonstrating strategic thinking: strategic plans, marketing frameworks, or client presentations you developed (redacted as needed for confidentiality)  
Responses to application questions  


Applications without strategic work samples will not be considered.  


Marketing Direction is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Director, Growth Strategy
description:
### **About Darkroom**

**Darkroom** is the leading next\-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.


At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by **Shadow**, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive\-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.


Our track record speaks for itself: **billions in attributable revenue** driven across e\-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.


What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the **fastest\-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000\)** and among the **most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos)**. Our founders were recognized by **Forbes 30 Under 30** for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.

### **About the Role**


As a **Director of Growth Strategy**, you will be responsible for a small portfolio of game\-changing brands that have engaged with Darkroom to accelerate their growth. You are one\-half management consultant — spending your day dissecting inefficiencies within the business and marketing programs — and one\-half marketing strategy leader, building and orchestrating a Darkroom team meant to deliver growth. This is a client\-facing position meant for the best and brightest in growth marketing and media strategy, capable of leveraging their unique skillset.


Perfect candidates will be obsessed with growth strategy for consumer brands, experts at deploying efficient omni\-channel paid advertising campaigns that deliver impact, with particular expertise in and knowledge growing the DTC channel at large. You should be comfortably managing the go\-to\-market for fast\-growth emerging brands pre\-revenue as equally as managing a $50M DTC P\&L for a household consumer brand.


This position is the most senior position on a Darkroom omni\-channel client engagement (meaning the client may be working with us across multiple sales channels — Amazon, DTC, TikTok Shop, Retail). Being successful in this role requires mastery of the DTC function, a willingness to understand (and master) how integrated paid media drives growth impact on the other channels (Amazon, Retail), and effective communication across teams and departments. You will be Directly managing the relationship with the client, scaling ad accounts upwards of $1M USD per month, and having excellent client relationship management skills. You live and growth strategy, funnel construction, and performance marketing. You want to be a part of the best team in the business at scaling brands through digital advertising.


You’ll report to the Managing Director of the department and play a key role in scaling Darkroom’s footprint across a portfolio of high\-growth consumer brands. You’ll be responsible for owning full funnel advertising efforts, including weekly client team touch\-bases and QBRs, while working closely with all Darkroom teams to ensure performance goals are met.


This is a fast\-paced, high\-accountability environment designed for marketers who are ambitious, curious, and ready to grow. You’ll gain hands\-on experience in a broad range of verticals, while developing the skills needed to be a future CMO.


Darkroom is a career incubator for ambitious talent looking to operate at the intersection of business, design, and innovation.

**What you’ll do:**

* Develop integrated growth strategies that deliver millions in yearly growth for clients.
* Developing campaigns where all marketing channels are working together and be able to clearly articulate overall themes as well as trends across the paid media plan.
* Work on financial projections, budgets, P\&L, and other financial documents to be shared internally, with the client, and used as a north star for work.
* Work with our data team to deliver financial projections, customer cohort analyses, and media plans.
* Work with Darkroom Finance team to project client spend and increase it based on performance.
* Leverage these data insights to develop unique marketing strategies across various industries.
* Lead multiple teams of designers, paid acquisition marketers, retention marketers, and data analysts across the globe for your client portfolio.
* Work with Darkroom success to develop fractional and dedicated marketing org charts based on client needs.

**You should apply if this sounds like you:**

* 10\+ years of paid media experience with a minimum of 7 years in DTC or E\-Commerce.
* You are a master of the DTC P\&L and are comfortable engaging in discussions around contribution margin, new customer revenue, and repeat revenue.
* You understand revenue is a derivative of AOV, CVR, CPM, Traffic and can articulate this in a marketing pro\-forma.
* You have a great willingness to understand (and master) how integrated paid media drives growth impact on the other channels (Amazon, Retail), not just DTC.
* You are a master of constructing e\-commerce funnels that include irresistible offers, healthy AOV, and speak to the UVPs of a product. You can build creative strategies off these funnels and communicate them clearly to the client.
* You are excited by complex challenges that require innovative marketing strategies to unlock scale.
* Your 5\-10 year plan has you becoming a CMO or CEO of your own brand.
* You are excited by the challenge to work at the intersection of growth marketing, creative advertising, and financial analysis.
* You have an acute attention to detail and are capable of project managing large teams with various functions.
* You are able to be flexible, understand shifting client needs, deal with ambiguity, and adapt to the situation.
* You are not afraid to be a leader and deliver thoughtful direction that keeps projects on track.
* You are proficient in financial projections, dealing with P/Ls, and holding your own with Private Equity firms, CEOs, CFOs, or a management team when challenged to defend your strategies surrounding marketing movements and paths to scale.
* You are excited to work with innovative data models that give you unique insights into product, customer, and marketing data. You thrive on this data to develop new strategies.
* Know the entire acquisition marketing landscape, and have a perspective on how to build world\-class campaigns in today’s changing landscape – this includes knowledge of the tools to use, running effective campaigns, the metrics to measure, and the proper attribution methodologies
* You like building teams and are comfortable being the quarterback, directing retention, paid media, creative, and data teams in harmony to deliver efficient growth.
* Experience in online sales channel strategies; Shopify, Amazon, third\-party fulfillment, and wholesale.
* You understand media measurement and the differences between MTA, MMM, and In Platform attribution. You are keen to figure out how best to think about measurement for your clients.

**What It's Like to Work at Darkroom**


Darkroom is not a typical agency. We are looking for A players who want to build something great. We move fast, think deeply, and expect every team member to bring insight and rigor to everything they touch. We hold a high bar for performance, creativity, and ownership — but we also support each other relentlessly. No egos, no red tape — just world\-class talent building something remarkable.


We believe in autonomy with accountability, truth over comfort, and outcomes over optics.

* **Unlimited PTO \+ US holidays:** Rebooting is part of the work. Take the time you need to stay sharp.
* **Remote\-First Culture:** Many roles are fully remote. Employees based in or near our New York or Lisbon HQs are expected to work hybrid with weekly in\-office time. Hub locations include the Brazil and Spain.
* **Health \& Wellness:** Company\-sponsored medical, dental, and vision coverage.
* **Finances, growth, and retirement:** Darkroom offers a robust 401(k) program with company match and profit\-sharing opportunities to help you save and grow as the company grows.
* **Parental Leave:** Flexible parental leave to support new parents during this important transition.
* **Growth:** Our interdisciplinary model gives every Darkroomer exposure far beyond their core role. Grow your skills, expand your influence, and stay at the forefront of the industry.

**Our Hiring Process and Compensation:**


The hiring process for this position typically follows three stages; phone screen, hiring manager, and Executive calls.

**Equal Opportunity Statement**


Darkroom is an equal opportunity workplace—we are dedicated to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national orientation, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements

title: Fractional Chief Revenue Officer (CRO)
description:
**About Plutus Health Inc.:**  

Plutus Health Inc. is a leading provider of Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) services with SOC2 Certification. We are dedicated to helping healthcare providers improve their financial performance. Our expertise spans across various specialties. We are committed to delivering exceptional service and innovative solutions to our clients. As a result, **Plutus Health Inc. has been recognized on the 5000 list of the fastest\-growing private companies in America and ranked 100 in the Dallas area. Additionally, the company has been a 2024 finalist in the EY Entrepreneur Of The Year.**

  

Position Overview:
  

The Fractional Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) will be responsible for overseeing all revenue\-generating activities within Plutus Health Inc. This C\-suite executive will develop and implement strategies to increase sales, expand market presence, and maximize revenue opportunities. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record in driving revenue growth within the healthcare or RCM industry.  

  

Key Responsibilities:  

  

1\. Revenue Growth Strategy:  

* Develop and execute comprehensive revenue generation strategies that align with the company’s overall goals.
* Identify new market opportunities and lead initiatives to capture additional market share.
* Drive sales growth through effective sales strategies, partnerships, and business development activities.

  

2\. Sales Leadership:  

* Lead, mentor, and develop the sales team to achieve and exceed revenue targets.
* Establish and monitor sales performance metrics, ensuring accountability and continuous improvement.
* Foster a high\-performance sales culture focused on customer satisfaction and revenue growth.

  

3\. Market Expansion:  

* Identify and evaluate new market segments and customer needs.
* Develop and implement strategies to enter new markets and expand the company’s footprint.
* Collaborate with marketing to create targeted campaigns that drive demand and brand awareness.

  

4\. Client Relationship Management:  

* Build and maintain strong relationships with key clients and partners.
* Ensure high levels of client satisfaction and retention.
* Develop strategies to upsell and cross\-sell services to existing clients.

  

5\. Revenue Operations:  

* Oversee the development and implementation of pricing strategies.
* Collaborate with finance to forecast revenue, set targets, and monitor performance against goals.
* Ensure the alignment of sales, marketing, and operations to drive revenue growth.

  

6\. Data\-Driven Decision Making:  

* Utilize data analytics to inform strategic decisions and optimize revenue\-generating activities.
* Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and industry developments to stay ahead of the curve.
* Provide regular reports and updates to the executive team on revenue performance and opportunities.

  

Qualifications:  

  

* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Finance, or a related field.
* Minimum of 10 years of experience in revenue generation roles, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership position.
* Proven track record of driving revenue growth in the healthcare or RCM industry.
* Strong strategic thinking and analytical skills.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
* Demonstrated ability to lead and inspire high\-performing sales teams.
* Proficiency in CRM software and data analytics tools.

  

Why Plutus Health Inc.:  

  

* Opportunity to work with a dynamic and innovative team.
* Play a key role in shaping the revenue strategy of a growing company.
* Competitive compensation and benefits package.

title: Fractional CTO (part-time)
description:
On behalf of our Client from the USA, Mobilunity is looking for a Fractional CTO for a part\-time engagement.



Our Client is a procurement consulting and managed services firm transforming its core intelligence into CCM Synq, an AI\-native vendor governance decision platform for mid\-market and enterprise organizations.



We are seeking a builder\-first Fractional CTO who will act as the senior technical voice for the platform. The focus is on translating CCM’s consulting and domain expertise into a scalable, AI\-native SaaS platform capable of proactive monitoring, alerting, and autonomous action.



Responsibilities:


* Conduct a technical audit of the current platform, codebase, infrastructure, and data model.
* Define the architecture for a multi\-tenant B2B SaaS platform.
* Assess AI/LLM integration, agentic workflows, contract ingestion, and vendor intelligence pipelines.
* Drive the transition from reporting/dashboard to proactive, agent\-driven platform capabilities.
* Recommend build vs. buy vs. integrate decisions, including AI orchestration frameworks.
* Own the technical roadmap and oversee any external development teams or agencies.
* Lead integration strategy across ERP, finance, and workflow systems (NetSuite, QuickBooks, Workday, Aderant, Elite 3E).
* Serve as technical voice for prospects, partners, and investors.


Requirements:


* Proven experience standing up at least 2 B2B SaaS platforms from early stage.
* Hands\-on experience with AI/LLM integration, document analysis, RAG pipelines, or agentic workflows.
* Familiarity with AI orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen, or similar).
* Deep understanding of multi\-tenancy, RBAC, and enterprise data security (SOC 2 readiness is a plus).
* Experience designing systems that ingest and structure unstructured data (contracts, invoices, vendor documents).
* Strong integration experience (ERP systems, REST APIs, OAuth, webhooks).
* Proven ability to lead/manage remote development teams or agencies.
* Clear, direct communication with non\-technical founders and stakeholders.


In return we offer:


* The friendliest community of like\-minded IT\-people
* Open knowledge\-sharing environment – exclusive access to a rich pool of colleagues willing to share their endless insights into the broadest variety of modern technologies
* No open\-spaces setup – separate rooms for every team’s comfort and multiple lounge and gaming zones on all of the 3 floors
* English classes in 1\-to\-1 \& group modes with elements of gamification
* Neverending fun: sports events, tournaments, music band, multiple affinity groups


Come on board, and let’s grow together!

title: Recruitment & Client Success Lead
description:
***Link to apply listed at the bottom of this Job Description. Only applications submitted through this link will be considered.***

**WHO WE ARE**

SpyGlass Ops is an operations consultancy that helps purpose\-based leaders scale themselves and their visions through operations. Founded by a former COO turned consultant, we work in the messy middle of business transformation — helping founders navigate the human side of scaling through deep business inspections, operations recruitment, and leadership coaching. We've helped dozens of 7 and 8\-figure businesses build the operational foundations they need to grow beyond the sum of their parts.

Our mission is helping purpose\-based leaders scale themselves and their visions through operations. We define operations as optimizing a company's time, energy, money, physical resources, and human potential — and we believe it's the highest leverage point for transformation.

Our Core Values:

* **Play Like A Nerd:** We are a team of nerds — each hopelessly passionate and excited about our area of expertise. We believe that if you are an absolute nerd for what you do, then work is play. And we play hard.
* **Own Your Opportunities:** Every mistake or failure is an opportunity — see it and use it as such. Take as much ownership as you possibly can and use it as feedback to improve. The only two words you will never hear at this company: fault and blame.
* **Commit to Your Own Potential:** We expect this of ourselves, each other, and our clients — to relentlessly, critically be redefining and growing into the most potent version of ourselves and the impact we can have.
* **Van Talk:** We are all in this van together, let's talk about the butter. Van Talk is not just a willingness and ability to speak honestly. More importantly, it is a way of receiving communication — not as a personal affront, but as a genuine and necessary act of service to accomplish our shared goals and highest potential.

\*\*If our culture and mission does not resonate with you, please do not bother applying.

**JOB DESCRIPTION**

The SpyGlass Ops team is looking for an experienced Recruitment \& Client Success Lead with a proven track record in full\-cycle recruitment to own our client hiring process from end to end. This role combines recruitment leadership with client relationship management — you'll build and run hiring funnels that place top operations leaders into growing businesses, then nurture those client relationships to drive long\-term engagement with SpyGlass.

This is not a traditional recruiting role. You are not filling temp positions or sourcing off a job board and hoping for the best. You are executing a proven, matchmaker\-style hiring process for a very specific niche — placing COOs, Heads of Operations, Directors of Ops, and Operations Managers into 7 and 8\-figure businesses going through real transformation. Every placement you make directly impacts whether a founder can step back and grow the business they actually want to build, or whether the business stalls. The application code word is matchmaker.

This role will be central to SpyGlass's growth as we scale our recruitment capacity and deepen our client relationships. You will work closely with our Head of Product and alongside our fractional COO/inspector team who provide the strategic context for each hire, while you own the execution of finding and placing the right operator.

This is a full\-time, remote 1099 contractor position. Core working hours are 9am to 5pm EST, with mandatory availability from 11am to 3pm EST for internal meetings. This is a critical position within the business, as such, we host a rigorous, 4\-part application process.

**WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE UNIQUE**

You are stepping into a role where the system is already built and proven — the hiring methodology, the asset templates, the candidate evaluation framework, the onboarding process. What we need is someone who can own and scale it.

Your day\-to\-day will move between two worlds. On the recruitment side, you'll receive detailed role requirements from our inspectors (fractional COOs who have done deep diagnostic work inside each client's business), and from there you own the entire hiring funnel — building the assets with our product team, sourcing and screening candidates, conducting interviews, presenting finalists, and helping close offers. On the client success side, you'll manage the ongoing relationship with our clients, ensuring they continue to see value in partnering with SpyGlass beyond the initial placement.

**Your success will be measured by two key outcomes: placing the right operators into the right businesses consistently, and retaining clients who want to keep working with SpyGlass long\-term.** You'll work with leadership to establish clear 30/60/90\-day milestones during your first quarter.

This is a great fit for someone coming from a corporate or agency recruitment environment who wants the autonomy to own an entire recruitment division rather than just being a cog in someone else's machine — while also being part of a team that genuinely cares about the work and each other.

**RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Recruitment Leadership**

* Own the full\-cycle recruitment process for operations leadership placements (COO, Head of Ops, Director of Ops, Operations Manager)
* Onboard new clients into the recruitment process, attend Report of Findings calls led by inspectors, and take over the hiring process from that point forward
* Oversee the creation of hiring assets (job descriptions, skill assessments, interview question banks) working directly with our product assistant
* Source, screen, and interview top candidates using SpyGlass's proven methodology
* Present top candidates to clients with clear recommendations and supporting rationale
* Help structure and close offers, including managing candidate buy\-in through the finish line
* Hand off placed operators to the inspection team for onboarding support

**Client Success \& Account Management**

* Own the client retainer relationship post\-placement, driving continued engagement with SpyGlass services
* Manage client expectations and communication at key recruitment milestones (kickoff, candidate presentations, offer negotiation)
* Identify opportunities for clients to continue working with SpyGlass and drive retainer renewals
* Serve as a reliable point of contact for client needs related to recruitment and account continuity

**Process \& Data Ownership**

* Leverage data from the recruitment process to identify improvements and inform strategy
* Present process changes and recommendations backed by data, not feeling
* Maintain and evolve recruitment workflows within our existing systems
* Scale the recruitment function from current volume to 25\+ concurrent engagements

**REQUIREMENTS**

**Experience Requirements (Non\-Negotiable)**

* Proven track record in full\-cycle/360 recruitment within a corporate or agency environment — this is not a role for someone coming from internal talent acquisition or staffing
* Experience recruiting for salaried, permanent positions — not temporary or contract staffing
* Demonstrated ability to manage client relationships and communicate professionally with business owners and leadership teams
* Experience managing candidates through a complete hiring process including sourcing, screening, interviewing, presenting, and closing
* Self\-starter who manages their own calendar, pipeline, and deadlines without needing to be told what to do next

**Core Competencies**

* Exceptional interviewing and candidate evaluation skills with the ability to distinguish between someone who talks a good game and someone who has genuinely done the work
* Strong client communication skills — you can set expectations, deliver difficult news, and maintain trust throughout the process
* Analytical mindset with the ability to use data to inform decisions and present recommendations
* Project management capability to juggle multiple concurrent recruitments at different stages without dropping balls
* Comfort working with AI tools and leveraging technology to improve efficiency and output quality

**Nice\-to\-Have Experience**

* Recruitment experience specifically in operations, executive, or leadership\-level roles
* Account management, client success, or retainer\-based relationship management experience
* Familiarity with ClickUp, ATS platforms, Google Workspace, Zoom, and Loom

**Mindset \& Cultural Fit**

* You hold yourself accountable to the highest standards of performance and are resilient in the face of obstacles or failure
* You thrive in a fast\-paced startup environment where you have real ownership and real impact — not just a seat at someone else's table
* You are fulfilled by the matchmaking element of recruitment — finding the right person for the right role genuinely excites you
* You are comfortable in an outcome\-based environment where nobody tracks your hours or micromanages your inputs, but your results speak for themselves
* You are a direct, honest communicator who values transparency and can both give and receive candid feedback
* You want to work hard, be recognized for your contributions, and then go enjoy your life

**BENEFITS**

* Compensation: $4000 to $6000 base \+ performance bonuses, on\-track earnings upward of $100K per year.
* 1099 Contractor relationship providing maximum flexibility and autonomy in how you structure your work
* Remote position with core working hours of 9am\-5pm EST
* Outcome\-based culture — we measure results, not hours
* Professional Development: Access to SpyGlass's operations coaching curriculum and leadership development resources to grow your own operational skill set
* The opportunity to own and build a recruitment division within a growing, purpose\-driven company

**HOW TO APPLY**

If you are interested in this position, please submit an application at the following link.

**https://eltiunaqqfh.typeform.com/to/lpwGk5Nv**

Our team will review your application and get back to you shortly. We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Inspector/ Fractional COO
description:
***Link to apply listed at the bottom of this Job Description. Only applications submitted through this link will be considered.***

**WHO WE ARE**

SpyGlass Ops is an operations consulting firm that helps purpose\-driven leaders scale themselves and their visions through operations. Founded by Jhana Li — a former COO who saw the same pattern across dozens of 7 and 8\-figure businesses — SpyGlass Ops works in the messy middle of business transformation, helping founders navigate the human side of scaling.

We don't do cookie\-cutter consulting. We conduct deep\-dive business inspections, place top\-tier operational leaders, and provide hands\-on fractional COO services that transform how companies operate from the inside out. Our clients are high\-achieving digital entrepreneurs building education companies, coaching businesses, agencies, and professional service organizations — and they come to us because they're stuck, scaling faster than their infrastructure can support, and ready to do the hard work of real transformation.

Our mission is to help purpose\-based leaders scale themselves and their visions through operations — because we believe operations is the highest leverage point for transformation when it's done right.

Our Core Values:

* **Play Like A Nerd:** We are a team of nerds — each hopelessly passionate and excited about our area of expertise. We believe that if you are an absolute nerd for what you do, then Work is Play. And we play hard.
* **Own Your Opportunities:** Every mistake and failure is an opportunity — see it and use it as such. Take as much ownership as you possibly can, and use it as feedback to improve. The only two words you will never hear at this company: fault and blame.
* **Commit to Your Own Potential:** We expect this of ourselves, each other, and our clients — to relentlessly, critically be redefining and growing into the most potent version of ourselves and the impact we can have.
* **Van Talk:** We are all in this van together, let's talk about the butter. Van Talk is not just a willingness and ability to speak honestly. More importantly, it is a way of receiving communication — not as a personal affront, but as a genuine and necessary act of service to accomplish our shared goals and highest potential.

\*\*If our culture and mission does not resonate with you, please do not bother applying.

**JOB DESCRIPTION**

SpyGlass Ops is looking for a seasoned COO turned strategic advisor to join our team as an **Inspector / Fractional COO**. This is not a traditional operations role — and it's not a traditional consulting role either. This is the primary client\-facing delivery position at SpyGlass Ops, and it sits at the intersection of deep business diagnostics, executive coaching, and operational transformation.

You will lead our signature deep\-dive business inspections — analyzing data, conducting interviews, identifying root causes, and delivering comprehensive reports of findings that change how founders see their own companies. From there, you'll transition into ongoing fractional COO work with a portfolio of clients, serving as their strategic advisor and transformation coach. Your job is to untangle the human webs that prevent organizations from scaling — management gaps, delegation failures, role confusion, founder bottlenecks, cultural drift, and strategic misalignment — and coach leaders through the messy middle of building something better.

The application code word is CATALYST. You will not be asked to sell, handle billing, lead recruitment execution, or build systems inside client businesses. Your focus is entirely on diagnostics, strategy, and the human side of operations — supported by a team that handles everything else.

This is a full\-time, remote, 1099 contractor position. Given the critical nature of this role, we host a rigorous, multi\-part application process.

**WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE UNIQUE**

This is not a fractional COO engagement where you're embedded in one company part\-time. At SpyGlass, you are the diagnostic engine and transformation coach for a portfolio of purpose\-driven businesses, each at a different stage of their operational evolution.

Your work begins with the inspection — a deep\-dive analysis across every dimension of a client's business. You'll review financial data, conduct team interviews, assess systems and processes, evaluate leadership dynamics, and synthesize everything into a clear picture of what's really going on beneath the surface. Then you'll present those findings, build an operator profile for the recruitment team, and transition into the ongoing advisory relationship.

The fractional COO work is where the real transformation happens. You'll coach founders through extracting themselves from day\-to\-day operations. You'll help build strategic vision, establish role clarity, create job scorecards, roll out OKRs, and navigate the conversations that nobody else in the organization is willing to have. Some weeks that means helping a CEO see that they're the bottleneck. Other weeks it means mediating a leadership conflict or walking a founder through a difficult termination decision.

You are supported by SpyGlass's product team, account management team, and recruitment team — so you can focus entirely on what you do best: seeing what others can't, saying what others won't, and helping leaders build organizations worthy of their vision.

At full capacity, you'll manage approximately 3 inspections per month alongside a portfolio of 10–15 active fractional COO clients.

**RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Business Inspection \& Diagnostics**

* Conduct deep\-dive business inspections across SpyGlass's Six Pillars: Team, Leadership, Mindset, Strategy, Systems, and Data
* Review all client\-submitted data — financials, org charts, survey results, CRM data, meeting recordings — and identify patterns, gaps, and root causes
* Conduct structured interviews with team members, department heads, and CEOs to surface operational realities beneath the surface narrative
* Synthesize findings into a comprehensive inspection report with clear root cause identification, financial impact analysis, and prioritized recommendations
* Build and deliver the report of findings presentation to clients
* Construct detailed operator profiles when a hire is necessary — calibrated to the specific needs, dynamics, and growth stage of each client organization

**Fractional COO \& Strategic Advisory**

* Serve as the ongoing strategic advisor and transformation coach for an assigned portfolio of clients
* Lead strategic vision development — helping founders articulate a grounded North Star and reverse\-engineer it into actionable roadmaps, quarterly priorities, and departmental goals
* Conduct role clarity exercises, build job scorecards with lever\-based KPIs, and redesign org structures for scalability
* Perform time analysis and EHV (Entrepreneurial Highest Value) work to help founders focus on their highest\-leverage activities and delegate effectively
* Roll out OKRs and accountability frameworks that connect individual performance to company\-level objectives
* Lead operator onboarding alongside the client to set placed candidates up for success

**Founder Coaching \& Change Management**

* Coach founders through the transformation process — holding accountability, calling out blind spots, and navigating resistance to change with diplomacy and directness
* Facilitate hard conversations within client organizations including performance issues, leadership dynamics, and personnel decisions
* Serve as a stabilizing, clarifying force for leadership teams experiencing the discomfort of meaningful organizational change
* Manage the human side of transformation — helping teams adapt to new standards, structures, and expectations without losing momentum or trust

**Internal Collaboration**

* Collaborate with SpyGlass's product team on execution support and process improvement
* Hand off operator profiles and hiring context to the recruitment team with clear, actionable documentation
* Coordinate with account management on client onboarding, scheduling, and engagement continuity

**REQUIREMENTS**

**Experience (Non\-Negotiable)**

* Senior operations leadership experience — you have held a Head of Operations, Director of Operations, VP of Operations, or COO title inside a growing business and have real experience managing teams, building infrastructure, and driving organizational change
* Direct experience coaching or advising founders and CEOs — you have navigated strong personalities, held accountability conversations, managed up, and helped visionaries extract themselves from day\-to\-day operations
* Track record leading organizations through operational transformation and change management — you have implemented new standards, restructured teams, shifted cultural norms, and managed the human resistance that accompanies meaningful change
* Cross\-functional business acumen — you have operated across or overseen multiple departments (sales, marketing, operations, finance, fulfillment) and understand how they interconnect
* Financial literacy — you can read a P\&L, spot red flags, assess whether a business model can achieve its stated goals, and use financial data to inform strategic decisions
* Experience with organizational design — you have built or restructured org charts, defined role clarity, and created accountability frameworks tied to measurable outcomes

**Core Competencies**

* Diagnostic and analytical thinking — the ability to take a mountain of raw information and synthesize it into a clear, prioritized picture of root causes and recommended actions
* Strategic planning and execution — translating vision into actionable roadmaps, OKRs, quarterly priorities, and departmental goals
* Communication architecture — evaluating and redesigning how companies communicate, meet, and cascade information across levels
* Systems and process assessment — evaluating CRM usage, project management architecture, workflow design, and automation opportunities (you assess and advise; you don't build)
* Data and KPI strategy — understanding how metrics should be collected, displayed, and used for decision\-making at every level of an organization
* Cultural auditing — discerning whether core values are actively lived or just words on a wall, and identifying toxic patterns or psychological safety gaps

**Critical Soft Skills (Deal\-Breakers)**

* Accountability and directness — you have the confidence to tell a CEO what nobody else will, respectfully but without flinching
* High emotional intelligence — you read rooms, navigate egos, adapt your communication style, and build trust even in high\-tension environments
* Executive presence — you command respect with founders and C\-suite leaders through clarity, confidence, and brevity
* Radical curiosity — you ask the questions that peel back layers rather than accepting surface\-level answers
* Resilience and self\-direction — you manage your own schedule, client load, and priorities across multiple businesses without constant oversight
* Coaching orientation — you find genuine fulfillment in helping others break through barriers, even when the conversations are uncomfortable

**Strongly Preferred**

* Fractional, consulting, or multi\-client advisory experience — comfort with context\-switching across different businesses, managing competing priorities, and maintaining quality at scale
* Experience with EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating System) or similar operating frameworks
* Familiarity with common client tech stacks (ClickUp, Go High Level, Monday.com, Zapier, Make)
* Background working with digital businesses, education or coaching companies, or service\-based organizations in the 7–8 figure range
* Experience with personality or behavioral frameworks (DiSC, Myers\-Briggs, Enneagram)

**BENEFITS**

* Compensation of $8,000 to $12,000\+ per month USD, with predictable and transparent pay structure (base salary \+ hourly inputs \+ standard inspection fees)
* 1099 contractor relationship providing full autonomy in how you structure your work
* 100% remote — work from anywhere
* Full support infrastructure — you focus on client delivery while our product, account management, and recruitment teams handle everything else
* Professional development through SpyGlass's internal training, mentorship, and leadership resources
* A portfolio of purpose\-driven clients doing meaningful work — you're not optimizing ad spend or shipping widgets, you're helping leaders build organizations that change lives

**HOW TO APPLY**

If you are interested in this position, please submit an application at the following link.

**https://eltiunaqqfh.typeform.com/to/jXlcjb4e**

Our team will review your application and get back to you shortly. We look forward to hearing from you!

Pay: $8,000\.00 \- $12,000\.00 per month

Benefits:

* Professional development assistance

Work Location: Remote
