title: Fractional Senior Accountant
description:
**About Ampleo**

**Cross\-Functional Leadership and Execution to Fuel Growth**

**We provide the expertise to accelerate your business at every stage and in every function.**

**Since 1996, Amplēo has helped thousands of businesses grow through on\-demand finance leadership, starting with fractional CFOs and outsourced controllers. What began as strategic financial support has evolved into a complete suite of cross\-functional services.**

**Today, Amplēo delivers embedded expertise in finance, HR, marketing, turnaround and restructuring, sales tax, and business valuation. Our professionals integrate directly into your team to provide both strategic insight and hands\-on execution, helping you achieve meaningful results faster.**

**We don’t just advise. We help you move, build, and win.**

 **Position Summary**


We are seeking an experienced Fractional Senior Accountant to take complete ownership of the accounting function from beginning to end. This is a high\-autonomy role where you are the go\-to person, not a support function, making it ideal for a self\-starter who thrives with independence and accountability.


In this role, you will be responsible for preparing and posting journal entries, performing account reconciliations, and generating accurate financial reports. Beyond the numbers, you will serve as a direct point of contact for clients, communicating findings and updates in a clear and professional manner. The ideal candidate brings a blend of technical accounting expertise, hands\-on operational experience, and the leadership skills necessary to manage and grow a dynamic accounting function.

 **Key Responsibilities**

* Own the full\-cycle accounting function, including journal entries, reconciliations, and financial reporting
* Communicate directly with clients regarding accounting matters
* Manage multiple client accounts across assigned industries
* Drive continuous improvements in accounting technology, including ERP/CRM integrations and automation.
* Lead month\-end and year\-end close processes and prepare financial statements (P\&L, balance sheet, cash flow)
* Ensure compliance with GAAP standards and maintain strong internal controls
* Support external audits by preparing and organizing required documentation
* Assist with budget preparation, variance analysis, and tracking actuals vs. forecast
* Coordinate with external CPAs or tax advisors on sales tax, payroll tax, and income tax filings
* Identify and implement process improvements to increase efficiency across accounting workflows
* Review and provide guidance to junior accounting staff as needed

 **Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (CPA required).
* 4–6 years of progressive accounting experience
* Industry experience in SaaS/tech industries or restaurant/hospitality industries
* Strong technical accounting knowledge and multi\-entity consolidation.
* Hands\-on experience with large\-scale ERP/CRM systems
* Track record of supporting a fast\-growing, scaling company with evolving needs.
* Strong leadership, organizational, and problem\-solving skills with an operational mindset.
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate across teams and present to senior leadership.

 **What We Provide:**


Amplēo believes in providing top executive talent the flexibility to work when, where, and as often as they want. Most people who want this flexibility have to worry about finding clients, managing billing, and the headache of running their own business. We take the hassle out of that for you. We match your skills with a client and take care of billing and payment. In return, you do what you are exceptional at. Total win\-win!

title: Accounting Manager - Client Lead
description:
**Graphite Financial** is a growing boutique finance, accounting, tax, and strategy firm (www.graphitefinancial.com) built to help startup and high\-growth companies scale efficiently and sustainably. Graphite Financial is redefining the “finance department” for early\-stage companies and VC\-backed startups. We deliver high\-caliber accounting and finance leadership and support to entrepreneurs and management teams building scalable, high\-growth companies. Our clients are actively managing cash burn, measuring performance, reporting to investors, making capital allocation decisions, and preparing for fundraising. All of that takes expertise and time that founders don’t have and can’t afford to hire for. Graphite bridges the gap with flexible, fixed\-fee support packages that provide fractional leadership and operational support from CFOs, Controllers, FP\&A Managers, and Tax leaders.

  

Graphite is looking for an experienced and process\-driven Accounting Manager/Client Lead ready to step into a high\-impact leadership role, guiding the financial strategy for a portfolio of high\-growth startup clients. We are seeking a dynamic and strategic leader with a proven record of establishing strong financial controls and successfully scaling accounting operations. This is a hands\-on position that requires deep technical expertise, exceptional client relationship skills, and a passion for the fast\-paced world of startups.

 **What You'll Be Leading**

  

This role is the direct owner of the client relationship, often partnering directly with the CEOs and executive teams of innovative, rapidly scaling companies. You won't just oversee, you'll strategize, implement, and deliver the financial clarity essential for their growth.

* **Client Leadership**: Serve as the primary, high\-touch contact for clients, owning the relationship and providing strategic advice on high\-level accounting, managerial accounting, and financial activities.
* **Team Development**: Manage, coach, and mentor a team of Staff and Senior Accountants, providing detailed review of their work to ensure impeccable quality and accuracy in financial reporting.
* **Operational Excellence**: Oversee all accounting functions, including the end\-to\-end monthly close process, financial statement preparation, and ensuring the smooth execution of day\-to\-day transactional work.
* **Strategic Project Management**: Coordinate and lead all activities for critical ad hoc projects, including external audits, due diligence support, and complex technical accounting research.
* **Financial Insight**: Deliver accurate, timely, and actionable client financial reporting to drive key decision\-making. This includes sophisticated analysis of key financial metrics, trends, and variances.
* **Cross\-Functional Collaboration**: Partner closely with our internal Finance and Tax departments to ensure every client's financial need is met with an exceptional level of customer service.

**Who You Are**

  

You are a strategic operator with the drive to build robust financial infrastructure for the next generation of industry leaders across a diverse portfolio of startups.

* **Experience:** You bring approximately 8\+ years of relevant client facing accounting experience (public accounting or corporate accounting), with at least 2 years of experience managing a team.
* **Education**: A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Accounting is required. A CPA or MBA is highly preferred, but not mandatory.
* **Technical Expertise**: Expertise in Web3 (Crypto) accounting and/or Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) accounting is a plus.
* **System Proficiency:** Significant experience with core GL platforms like QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, or Sage Intacct, plus exposure to modern, tech\-forward systems (e.g., Bill, Ramp, Stripe, Gusto, Rippling, Cin7, Brex).
* **Communication**: Possess strong communication skills with the rare ability to translate complex accounting concepts into clear, concise language for clients, executives, and non\-financial stakeholders.
* **Mindset:** You are a team player comfortable in a fast\-paced environment, possessing a high degree of professionalism, motivation, and a genuine passion for working with growing startup companies

**Comprehensive Benefits \& Perks:**

* **Health \& Wellness:** Competitive Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage. 100% of the employee's health insurance premium is covered by the company.
* **Financial Security:** Company\-paid Short\-Term and Long\-Term Disability insurance.
* **Work/Life Balance To Include:** Generous Unlimited Paid Time Off (PTO), 11 Paid Company Holidays, and comprehensive Paid Parental Leave.
* **Remote Work Setup:** Fully remote position with company\-provided technology equipment and a $250 technology stipend to support your ideal home office setup.
* **401k:** With a company match


Compensation for this role is highly competitive and will be determined based on the successful applicant's experience with special consideration given to proven **Web3 (Crypto) and CPG accounting expertise** as well as geographic location.

 **The pay range for this role is:**  

96,050 \- 113,000 USD per year(Remote (United States))

title: Operations Hoʻokele
description:
**About QRSE:**


Founded in 2013, QRSE delivers construction management, environmental advisory, and engineering services for public infrastructure clients including state DOTs, FHWA, county governments, and federal consultants. We have four business lines:

* **Advisory \& Environmental Services**(national scope, led from the Midwest)
* **Construction Services \& Staffing**(Hawaii and federal/military)
* **E\-Project Delivery \& Technology Services**(AI\-assisted engineering tools that amplify the firm’s core expertise)
* **Program management**

**Our Culture:**


Our culture is grounded in five Hawaiian values: Pono (do right), ‘Ano Kūlana (quality), Ho‘omaopopo (understanding before action), Lōkahi (unity), and Le‘ale‘a (joy in serious work). We are small, distributed, and intentionally high\-caliber. We operate with a scrappy, technology\-forward mindset and value team players who are humble and willing to contribute wherever needed. We don’t manage people closely, we hire people who don’t need to be managed.


AI is a multiplier here, not a buzzword. We use Claude Code, ChatGPT, Power Automate, and other AI tools every day, for document creation, data analysis, process design, and operational automation. The Operations Hoʻokele will build the operational infrastructure that supports all four business lines, and AI is one of the primary tools you’ll use to do it. We expect you to be working proficiently in Claude Code within your first month. Not as a developer; as someone who uses it to do the work of this role faster and better than without it.

 **About the Role:**


QRSE is a 52\-person engineering consulting and construction management firm headquartered in Honolulu with staff across the mainland. Our technical delivery runs well. What we need now is a leader to build the operational backbone, establishing systems, workflows, and team management that enable the Executive Leadership Team (ELT) to stay focused on clients and growth. Currently, leadership is highly involved in day\-to\-day operations; the right person in this role will gradually shift that involvement toward a more strategic oversight function over time.


You’ll lead our Operations Hui, the team that handles finance, HR operations, payroll, marketing, training, and technology systems. You’ll manage the people who keep the company running and the people who build better ways to run it. You’ll use AI tools daily to make a small team punch above its weight.

**This is a hands\-on, working leadership role.** You will personally produce work, solve problems, and build systems, not just assign tasks and review what comes back. If your instinct is to delegate first and check in later, this isn’t the right fit. If your instinct is to dig in, figure out why something is broken, fix it, and then build a system so it doesn’t break again; keep reading.

 **About the Role:**

*This is not a job description full of corporate* *filler**.* You’ll lead the Operations division.


You’ll also coordinate part\-time on\-island staff on Oahu, and eventually Kauai, who handles physical tasks (mail, check deposits, document handling, supply orders). Your job is to build checklists and systems so these tasks run on autopilot without requiring you or the ELT to think about them.

*Here’s what your weeks will look like:*

**Run and Develop the Team**

* Set priorities, run regular check\-ins, hold people accountable to deadlines, and step in to do the work alongside them when things get busy.
* Develop employees at different levels: coordinators who need structure and mentorship, specialists who need strategic direction and autonomy, and a comptroller who needs a thought partner, not a micromanager.
* .
* Handles HR and performance issues directly. This team has operated without consistent leadership. You’ll need to set expectations, address performance where needed, and build a culture of accountability that didn’t exist before.

**Own Finance \& Payroll Operations**

* Chase down timesheets, mileage logs, and receipts across all staff; on time, every time.
* Work with the Comptroller on invoicing, AP/AR follow\-up, and cash flow reporting.
* Support budget preparation and cost monitoring for firm operations.
* Coordinate with external finance consultants and manage the relationship with auditors.
* Own certified payroll compliance and reporting.

**Handle HR \& Compliance**

* Run the new\-hire onboarding process end\-to\-end: offer letters, benefits enrollment, equipment, system access, first\-week orientation.
* Coordinate with our PEO partner (ADP) and manage 401(k) administration.
* Maintain DBE/WBE/MBE certification paperwork and stay ahead of renewal deadlines.
* Keep employee policies current and make sure the team is trained through the firm’s LMS (Litmos).
* Work with the fractional HR consultant on compliance, employee relations, policies, and over time, build enough internal capability to reduce that dependency.
**Support Marketing \& Business Development**

* Oversee the Marketing Coordinator on website updates, proposal production, internal resumes, and conference logistics.
* Track proposal deadlines and assemble submission packages with technical leads.
* Enforce brand consistency across QRSE’s three business lines in all external materials.

**Build Systems and Fix What’s Broken**

* Find operational bottlenecks, propose solutions, and implement them. You are expected to bring ideas, not wait for assignments.
* Collaborate with the Innovation team’s work on AI\-assisted tools for document generation, data analysis, reporting, scheduling, and internal communications.
* Manage vendor relationships, software subscriptions, insurance renewals, and contract administration.
* Standardize processes across the company, the stuff that’s been done differently by everyone because nobody owned it.

**Keep the ELT Focused**

* Take functions fully off the ELT’s plate. Right now, ELT members are filling gaps in finance oversight, HR decisions, and technology direction. Your job is to own these so they can stop.
* Prepare meeting agendas, track action items, produce status reports.
* Translate leadership decisions into operational follow\-through with clear owners and deadlines.
**Who Thrives in This Role**


We’re looking for someone early\-to\-mid career (5–10 years) in operations, office management, or business administration who still has the energy to build, the curiosity to learn new tools every month, and the resilience to manage competing priorities without someone telling them what to do next.

**You’ll succeed here if you:**

* **Build systems, not just run them.** You see how pieces connect, where processes break down, and you fix the root cause, not just the symptom.
* **Communication**. You communicate proactively sharing what you know, what you don't, and what you need before someone has to ask. You raise issues early. You stay calm and structured when things get messy. And you understand that your core job is to make sure the ELT is never the default answer to a question that should have an owner.
* **You don't sit on bad news**. If payroll is going to be late, a vendor relationship is deteriorating, or a team member isn't performing; you say so early, with context and a proposed path forward. We'd rather hear an uncomfortable truth on Monday than a crisis on Friday.
* **Prioritize and Ownership. This** role will rarely look the same week to week. A compliance deadline, a staffing gap, a system that breaks at the worst time, the work will interrupt itself constantly. What we need is someone who can hold a clear picture of what matters most, make confident calls about what moves and what doesn't, and keep the team oriented when the plan changes. If you need a stable to\-do list to do your best work, this isn't the right environment
* **Do the work yourself.** When something needs to get done, your first move is to doit; not assign it. You lead by producing, not by overseeing.
* **Use AI tools to get real work done.** You’ve used ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, or automation platforms to solve actual problems; not just experiment. We use Claude Code daily for document creation, data analysis, HR processes, and operational automation. You’ll be expected to be proficient within your first month. If that sounds exciting rather than intimidating, you’re our person.
* **Move fast and adjust.** You get up to speed quickly, say when you’re uncertain, treat mistakes as data, and try again. Ambiguity doesn’t slow you down.
* **Write clearly.** This is a hybrid role with remote employees. Clear emails, concise summaries, and coherent proposals are non\-negotiable.
* **Take feedback without taking it personally.** You have low ego and strong judgment. You speak up when you see something differently and have the reasoning to back it up and you adjust course quickly when the evidence points another way.

  

**This role probably isn’t for you if:*** Your management style is primarily delegation and oversight. We need someone who personally produces work every day, not someone who assigns it and reviews it.
* You prefer established processes to building new ones. Much of what you’ll manage here doesn’t exist yet. That’s the point.
* You’re skeptical of AI or prefer to work without it. AI is a daily tool here, not an optional add\-on. If picking up a tool like Claude Code in your first month sounds unreasonable, this isn’t the right fit.
* You need a lot of structure and clear direction to do your best work. We’ll give you goals and context, but you’ll need to figure out the “how” yourself.  

*We care far more about how you think than where you’ve worked.*

**What you bring**

**Required Qualifications:**

* 5–10 years in operations, office management, or business administration.
* Experience managing a small team (3–10 direct reports) where you also did the work, not just managed it.
* Experience with financial oversight, you don’t need to be the accountant, but you should be able to read a P\&L, manage a budget, and work credibly with a comptroller and auditors.
* Proficiency with Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and project management tools. Comfort learning new technology platforms quickly, you’ll be working in Claude Code, Power Automate, and other AI tools within your first month.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* The ability to remains calm, structured, and emotionally steady under pressure.
* Ability to work remotely during Hawaii business hours (HST, Mon–Fri).
**Preferred Qualifications:**

* Experience in A/E/C (architecture, engineering, construction) or professional services firms.
* Familiarity with government contract administration, DBE/WBE/MBE compliance, certified payroll, or federal procurement.
* Experience with Ajera/Deltekor similar project accounting software.
* Demonstrated use of AI productivity tools in a work setting\-Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, Power Automate, or similar. Show us in your application.
* HR generalist knowledge\-employment law basics, benefits administration, employee relations.
* Experience inheriting or building a team that didn’t have consistent leadership before you arrived.

**What You'll get:**

* **$60to $85/hour** with room to grow as the firm scales and your impact compounds.
* **A seat at the leadership table.** The Hoʻokele is a division leadership role at QRSE. You’ll participate in strategic planning, leadership meetings, and governance alongside the heads of Construction Management, Field Engineering, Inspection, Environmental, and Program divisions.
* **Direct mentorship from firm leadership** who will invest in developing you into the next generation of operational leaders.
* **Exposure to four business lines:** construction management, environmental advisory, AI\-enabled engineering tools, and Program Management; not a single vertical.
* **The chance to build from scratch.** QRSE knows it needs operational infrastructure. You’re not inheriting someone else’s system, you’re building yours.
* **AI as a real tool, not a talking point.** This team uses Claude, GPT, and automation tools every day to move faster. You’ll have the budget and support to do the same.
* **A distributed team** across Hawaii, California, Illinois, Virginia, Colorado, Missouri, Florida, and Pennsylvania.

**Why QRSE?****We Invest in Our People**
At QRSE, personal and professional growth isn’t an afterthought; it’s a priority. We believe that when we invest in our people, we empower them to do their best work and deliver exceptional results for our clients.**Our comprehensive benefits package includes:*** Competitive compensation
* 13 paid holidays
* 100% employer\-paid medical, dental, and vision (including dependents)
* 401(k) with company match (100% of first 3%, 50% of next 2%)
* Professional development, education, and certification reimbursement
* Wellness reimbursement
* Discounted perks(pet insurance, travel, entertainment, disability coverage)


Hourly Wage: $60 to $85 per hour
The hourly rate range listed reflects the expected pay for this position based on skills, experience, and internal equity. *QRSE, LLC is an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran status, or disability.*

title: Manager of Aviation
description:
**Manager of Aviation****Location: Fort Lauderdale, Florida (On\-site)****About the Role**
The Manager of Aviation is responsible for the strategic, operational, financial, and regulatory management of the family office aviation program. This leader will oversee flight operations, aircraft maintenance, crew management, safety programs, compliance frameworks, and vendor relationships.

**Role Overview**
The following are duties, responsibilities, and leadership roles that the Manager of Aviation shall provide:

**Aviation Strategy \& Program Leadership**

* Develop and execute the aviation strategy aligned with the principal’s travel requirements.
* Advise leadership on aircraft acquisition, leasing, upgrades, or fleet restructuring.
* Conduct mission analysis to determine optimal aircraft type, configuration, and utilization.
* Provide cost\-per\-flight\-hour analysis and lifecycle cost projections.
* Present periodic aviation performance, safety, and risk reports to leadership.

**Flight Operations \& Program Management**

* Oversee domestic and international flight operations across owned, leased, fractional, and charter aircraft.
* Ensure crew duty and rest compliance and operational readiness.
* Coordinate complex trip planning including international permits, fuel planning, and ground handling.
* Maintain relationships with global FBOs, aviation vendors, and service providers.
* Establish VIP protocols for principals, family members, guests, and security teams.

**Regulatory Compliance \& Safety Oversight**

* Ensure full compliance with FAA, ICAO, EASA, TSA, and international aviation regulations.
* Implement and maintain a robust Safety Management System (SMS).
* Maintain audit\-ready documentation and regulatory records.
* Oversee operational approvals, international flight permits, and compliance programs.
* Lead safety audits, risk assessments, and emergency response planning.

**Aircraft Maintenance \& Airworthiness**

* Oversee maintenance planning, heavy inspections, and aircraft reliability programs.
* Ensure compliance with airworthiness directives and service bulletins.
* Monitor maintenance tracking systems and warranty programs.
* Coordinate Aircraft on Ground (AOG) response planning and vendor support.

**Crew Leadership**

* Recruit, vet, and retain highly qualified aviation professionals.
* Oversee training programs, simulator scheduling, and performance standards.
* Foster a culture of safety, professionalism, and discretion.
* Maintain strict confidentiality and protocol standards.

**Financial Management**

* Develop and manage the annual aviation budget.
* Monitor cost\-per\-flight\-hour metrics and operational efficiency.
* Manage capital planning for aircraft maintenance, upgrades, or acquisitions.
* Negotiate and oversee aviation vendor contracts.

**Security Coordination**

* Partner with executive protection teams to coordinate aviation security protocols.
* Maintain aircraft access control and passenger manifest procedures.
* Coordinate secure ground transportation and travel logistics.

  

**Requirements** **Qualifications**


The Manager of Aviation requires a minimum of a bachelor’s degree with at least 10 years of experience in executive aviation leadership.

* Experience managing Part 135 operations.
* Strong background in international aviation operations.
* Experience supporting aircraft acquisition or fleet management.
* Deep knowledge of FAA regulations and international aviation standards.
* Strong financial acumen related to aviation cost modeling.
* Exceptional discretion and executive presence.

**Preferred**

* ATP (Airline Transport Pilot) license.
* CAM (Certified Aviation Manager) certification.
* Experience managing multi\-aircraft fleets.
* Experience supporting UHNW individuals or Fortune 100 executives.
* Experience with Gulfstream, Bombardier Global, or Hawker aircraft platforms.

 **Benefits**  



Outstanding Benefits




* 21 paid days off (15 days of PTO, which increases with tenure, plus 6 holidays).
* Flexible Spending Account (FSA) and Health Savings Account (HSA) options.
* Medical, dental, vision, long\-term disability, and life insurance.
* Generous 401(k) with up to 6% employer match.

  



At ICBD, we believe leadership, innovation, and purpose go hand in hand. Be part of a team where your work drives lasting impact and your career reaches new heights.

  



ICBD participates in the U.S. Department of Homeland Security E\-Verify program.

title: Accountant
description:
**Accountant**

**Reports To:** Executive Director

**Location:** On\-Site

**Status:** Full\-Time, Exempt

**Our Mission and Approach:**

HomeWoven’s mission is to revitalize neighborhoods where individuals and families can thrive for generations. Based in the Omaha metro, we believe that housing is more than just shelter; it’s the foundation for health, opportunity, and long\-term stability.

We provide various services, including no\-cost essential home repairs, homes for those looking to achieve the dream of homeownership, and homebuying assistance explicitly tailored for low\- to moderate\-income families in the vibrant Omaha metro. We're building a brighter future, one home at a time!

**Position Summary:**

We are seeking a detail\-oriented and experienced accountant to manage our daily financial operations. The ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining the integrity of our financial records using QuickBooks Online, executing accurate payroll, and ensuring compliance with grant reporting requirements. This person will play a critical role in providing financial transparency necessary for our organizational growth.

**Key Responsibilities:**

**Financial Management \& QuickBooks Oversight:**

* Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date financial records in QuickBooks Online.
* Perform monthly bank, credit card, and internal reconciliations.
* Manage Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR), ensuring timely payments and collections.
* Work with the Fractional CFO to prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements (P\&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow).
* Provide support for fundraising/development team (gift entry, financial reports)

**Payroll Administration:**

* Process semi\-monthly payrolls for all employees, ensuring accuracy in hours, deductions, and tax withholdings.
* Manage employee benefits, contributions, and payroll\-related taxes.
* Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local payroll regulations and filing requirements.
* Assist with the offboarding and onboarding processes for employees (tax, payroll, and I\-9 documentation).

**Grant Accounting \& Reporting:**

* Track expenditures against specific grant budgets to ensure compliance with donor requirements.
* Prepare and submit financial reports and reimbursement requests for private and government grants.
* Assist program managers in budget development and monitoring grant\-funded projects.
* Support Grant and Compliance Manager with grant applications

**General Accounting Operations:**

* Assist with year\-end audit preparation and tax filing requirements.
* Maintain an organized digital and physical filing system for all financial records.
* Identify and implement process improvements to streamline accounting workflows.
* Work with the Fractional CFO to ensure organizational efficiency and compliance

**Required Qualifications:**

* Education: Bachelor's degree in accounting, finance, or related field.
* Experience: 3–5 years of professional accounting experience.
* Technical Skills: Expert\-level proficiency in QuickBooks Online and Microsoft Excel (pivot tables), Bill.com, Bloomerang, and experience with payroll platforms (Paycor).
* Knowledge: Strong understanding of GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) and payroll tax laws.

**Preferred Skills:**

* Previous experience in nonprofit accounting.
* CPA or CMA certification is a plus.

**Core Competencies:** 

* High level of integrity and ability to handle confidential, sensitive information.
* Exceptional organizational skills and ability to meet strict deadlines.
* Analytical mindset with a high degree of accuracy and attention to detail.
* Ability to work with a team and meet deadlines.
* Possess strong communication skills.

**Nondiscrimination Policy:** 

At HomeWoven, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and welcoming environment for everyone. This includes our staff, clients, volunteers, subcontractors, vendors, and supporters. We firmly believe in the importance of diversity and equity, so we do not and will not tolerate discrimination in our activities or operations. We stand against bias based on race, color, religion (creed), gender, gender expression, age, national origin (ancestry), disability, marital status, sexual orientation, or military status. We strive to create a space where all individuals feel valued, respected, and empowered to contribute to our mission.

**Salary/Benefits Package:** 

HomeWoven is committed to pay transparency and equity. Salary is determined by a combination of experience, skills, and alignment with the role's responsibilities. The comprehensive benefits package encompasses health insurance, matching contributions to a 403(B)\-retirement plan, and paid time off.

If you're interested in applying, please send your cover letter and resume to Eric Crawford at eric@homewoven.org.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $65,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Director of Finance and Operations
description:
**Location:** Remote


**Reports to:** Chief Executive Officer


### **Position Summary**


One Firefly is seeking a Director of Finance and Operations to serve as the financial engine and operational oversight leader of the company. This is a high\-visibility, high\-impact role on our Executive Management Team (EMT) that sits at the intersection of financial discipline and operational efficiency in a service\-based business where human capital is our largest and important asset.


In this role, you will own the day\-to\-day financial operations of the business, driving forecasting and financial clarity. You will also provide operational oversight to our Project Management team and serve as the primary financial liaison to our fractional CFO.


This is an ideal opportunity for a sharp, financial\-forward individual with service industry experience who is ready to step into a true leadership seat and drive meaningful impact.



### **What You’ll Do**


* #### **Financial Operations \& Forecasting**


	+ Own and maintain accurate, timely financial reporting, delivering clear and actionable insight across the P\&L.
	+ Build, maintain and continuously improve rolling forecasts (revenue, margin and cash flow).
	+ Monitor daily cash flow and proactively manage our financial position, balancing liabilities, capital expenditures, and owner distributions.
	+ Drive the annual budgeting process and maintain ongoing budget\-vs\-actual tracking across all departments.
	+ Partner with Sales and Operations to ensure pipeline, onboarding and churn are accurately reflected in forecasts.
	+ Serve as primary liaison to the fractional CFO; preparing materials, surfacing risks, and ensuring clean data for strategic decision making.
	+ Partner with external CPA on tax\-related matters.
	+ Provide financial coaching and oversight to the Accounting team; serve as backup for transactional support when needed.
* #### **Project Management Oversight**


*As a service\-based business, human capital is our single largest cost driver. You will oversee the Project Management team with a financial and efficiency lens.*


	+ Lead and develop the Project Management team leads — providing coaching, goal\-setting, accountability and performance oversight.
	+ Ensure the team's labor and resource models are functioning effectively and that capacity decisions align with financial targets and forecast assumptions.
	+ Monitor utilization rates, billable\-to\-non\-billable ratios, and labor margin across service lines.
	+ Identify and escalate risks early, before they impact financial performance.
	+ Partner with the Executive Vice President to align labor efficiency with service quality and client outcomes.
* #### **Executive \& Cross\-Functional Leadership**


	+ Serve as an active member of our Executive Management Team (EMT).
	+ Partner with department leaders to drive budget accountability and financial decision\-making.
	+ Provide financial guidance on client\-related matters that have cost or margin implications.
	+ Support People Operations on payroll\-related financial approvals and workforce cost planning.
	+ Proactively identify risks, gaps and opportunities; this role is expected to run toward problems, not wait for them to surface.
### **What You Bring**


* 7\+ years of progressive finance experience, with at least 3 years at a Controller, Finance Manager or similar level.
* Background in a services\-based business (agency, consulting, staffing or managed services strongly preferred).
* Proven track record owning a financial close process and delivering clean, timely reporting.
* Experience leading and developing team members, including coaching, performance management, and driving accountability.
* Strong understanding of service business economics, including utilization, billable rates, capacity planning, and labor margin.
* Proven ability to build and maintain financial models, forecasts, and budget\-vs\-actual analyses.
* Strong communicator who can translate financial data into clear guidance for non\-finance stakeholders.
* Tactical and strategic use of AI to improve productivity and financial processes.
* High ownership mentality; treats the company's financial health as personally important.
* Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or related field; CPA is a plus.
* Experience with common agency accounting platforms (QuickBooks, NetSuite, or similar).
### **What We Offer**


**Compensation:** Total target cash compensation of $110,000 – $140,000 (Base \+ Performance Bonus).


* A remote\-first culture (Remote since 2015!).
* Private Health, Dental, and Vision 100% paid for employee only.
* 10 Paid Holidays and increasing PTO plus Volunteer days.
* Simple IRA with company match.
* Bonus opportunities tied to clear performance metrics.
* Personal Development Allowance.
* Company provided computer and office supply funds.
* Paid Parental Leave and in\-person team events.
### **Who We Are**


One Firefly is an award\-winning marketing agency serving the residential and commercial custom integration industry. Since 2007, we’ve helped technology professionals elevate their brands, attract qualified leads, and grow their businesses through a full suite of marketing services.


With a fully remote team distributed around the globe, we’re a people\-first company that values curiosity, creativity, and collaboration. Whether you're designing campaigns, optimizing strategies, or helping clients grow — we believe great work starts with great people.

title: Athlete Experience Lead, Super Age
description:
About Super Age \& The Role
Super Age is a new consumer brand focus on vital longevity – the lifestyle associated with living a long and happy life. Our flagship newsletter, *The Mindset*, has over 1M subscribers. This year we are launching two ambitious new offerings: a flagship IRL fitness competition called the Super Age Games, and a versatile training hub called The Super Age Standard. Both of these offerings will welcome a new type of community member into our world: Super Age athletes, people who are training and competing using our methodology, protocols, and objectives.


As the **Athlete Experience Lead** at Super Age, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the journeys of our athletes participating in our immersive IRL event – the Super Age Games – and our digital community training platform, The Standard. Your responsibility will be to ensure that every athlete feels valued, supported, and empowered as they engage with our brand, extending their healthspan through engagement without content and offerings, and enhancing their overall experience. **You will be both a partner to, and advocate for, our athletes as we design programming and offerings for them.**

  

This role requires a dedicated individual with a passion for athletics and longevity, an innate stance of hospitality and service mindset, and a love of experience design to help develop offerings that inspire and retain our athletes. You will work closely with our executive team, event production partner(s), and community leaders and brand allies, to fostering a culture of inclusivity and motivation for our athlete community.

  

What You Will Own in 2026  

1\. Athlete Onboarding and Engagement: Super Age Games* **Objective**: Create a seamless onboarding experience for athletes registering for the Super Age Games, including special attention to the Champion \[premium] pass holders.
* **Execution**: You will own and drive the development of onboarding materials, design and facilitate welcome flows and community experiences, and create personalized communication plans to engage athletes before, during, and after their event experience.


2\. The Standard: Engagement \& Development* **Objectives**: 1\) Own user communication and product tweaks to get our intended user behavior on our training platform, the Standard; 2\) Inform product development roadmap based on user behavior.
* **Execution**: Collaborate with our entire team to make sure our users are getting the value of the platform, and drive discussions and roadmapping about how we build out the platform to drive maximum value to our community.


3\. Community Building* **Goal**: Foster a strong community among our athletes that encourages support, friendship, and collaboration.
* **Execution**: Organize social events, create community forums, and facilitate group activities. Maintain engagement through newsletters, our consumer platform, and social media interactions.

  

**Requirements**  

* **Athletic Background**: You have a deep passion for athletics, sports participation, or coaching, as well as a strong understanding of athlete needs and experiences.
* **Experience Design \& Hospitality Expertise**: You have experience in designing and hosting events, and understand how small details shape one's experience, including digital events.
* **Consumer Product/App Experience:** You have experience owning product and/or community management workflows for a consumer app, ideally related to fitness. You have a strong sense of how to use user behavior data, user feedback, and product objectives to inform new features that lead to product engagement, momentum, and growth.
* **Strong Communication Skills**: Your verbal and written communication skills are excellent, enabling you to connect with athletes on various levels.


You can perform this role from anywhere in the USA, though a preference will be given to candidates located on the East Coast and those in/near New York City for closer collaboration with our executive team.

 **Benefits** **Autonomy:** You own the athlete experience function, because you are the athlete experience function. You will have a direct hand in bringing Super Age to life for thousands of people. This is a team where you will get the resources and authority that you need to work your magic, you will not be held back or micromanaged here.

 **Structure**. This role is intended to begin as a part\-time contract, before ramping into full\-time later this year or early next year. We are open to starting as full\-time for the perfect fit. For both contract/fractional and full time, pay will be a split between competitive base rate based on your background and performance bonuses based on milestones/outcomes.  

  

Once full\-time version of this role will have perks and benefits, including health insurance, vision, dental, and eligibility for equity etc.

title: Virtual Chief Security Officer (vCSO)
description:
We are seeking an experienced and strategic Virtual Chief Security Officer (vCSO) to lead and oversee NetGain contractual clientele’s cybersecurity program. The vCSO will be responsible for developing security strategies, advising leadership on risk management, ensuring compliance with relevant standards, and helping protect the organization’s systems, networks, data, and operations.


This role is ideal for a security professional who can combine hands\-on technical knowledge with executive\-level guidance and business alignment.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Develop, implement, and maintain the organization’s cybersecurity strategy, policies, and procedures
* Assess security posture and identify gaps, vulnerabilities, and areas for improvement
* Advise executive leadership on cybersecurity risks, incident preparedness, and security investments
* Oversee risk assessments, security audits, and remediation planning
* Guide security governance, compliance, and regulatory readiness efforts
* Support incident response planning, breach response coordination, and post\-incident analysis
* Monitor emerging threats and recommend appropriate controls and mitigation strategies
* Collaborate with IT, legal, compliance, and operational teams to align security with business objectives
* Review third\-party/vendor security risks and recommend safeguards
* Help define and maintain business continuity and disaster recovery security considerations
* Prepare reports, dashboards, and executive briefings on security performance and risk status
* Promote security awareness and best practices across the organization

**Required Qualifications**

* Minimum of 2 years of experience in IT security, cybersecurity, information security, or a related field
* Experience in security governance, risk management, compliance, and incident response
* Strong knowledge of network security, endpoint protection, identity and access management, cloud security, and security frameworks
* Demonstrated ability to communicate technical security concepts to non\-technical stakeholders and executive leadership
* Experience developing security policies, standards, and strategic roadmaps
* Strong analytical, organizational, and problem\-solving skills
* Ability to work independently in a virtual or fractional leadership capacity

**Minimum Certification Requirement**


Candidates must hold at least one of the following certifications:

* SSCP (Systems Security Certified Practitioner), or a comparable cybersecurity certification

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience serving as a vCISO, vCSO, Security Manager, or Information Security Consultant
* Familiarity with compliance standards such as NIST, ISO 27001, SOC 2, HIPAA, GLBA, or CMMC
* Experience with cloud environments such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud
* Relevant advanced certifications such as CISSP, CISM, CRISC, or CISA
* Experience in regulated industries or working with small to mid\-sized businesses

**Education**

* Bachelor’s degree in information security, Cybersecurity, Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field preferred
* Equivalent professional experience may be considered in lieu of formal education

**Work Environment**

* Remote/Virtual with some onsite work required (clientele mostly resides in the Arkansas (Little Rock) area.
* Full\-time

**Success in This Role**


A successful vCSO will provide practical and strategic security leadership, strengthen the organization’s security posture, reduce risk exposure, and help ensure the business is prepared for evolving cyber threats.
company_description: Since 1984, NetGain has been a leading provider of IT services and solutions, helping thousands of unique clients thrive. Serving business technology needs across the Midwest and Southeast U.S.

title: Capital Formation & Investor Relations Rep
description:
We're hiring a **Capital Formation \& Investor Relations Rep**!


* **Flexible Title:** Can tailor to reflect your skills \& experience.
* **Flexible Time:** Can do full\-time, part\-time, side\-gig (off\-hours), or fractional (contract).
* **Flexible Commitment:** Can do short\-term, long\-term, or intermittent.


**Why so flexible?** We're racing to launch in 60 days. Feel free to jump in, help us ship, then bounce \>\> or stick around. A successful launch translates into lots of permanent jobs for those that want them. We're also interested in long\-term "side gig" relationships — in our experience, a few expert hours often beat full\-time learning\-curve hours.


### **About The Role**


You're looking for investors. Work your network. Reach out, book calls, ask for referrals. The trick: ask for introductions, not money. Those who are interested will raise their hand.


Accredited investors \= $200k\+ income or $1M\+ net worth; doctors, execs, professionals. You know several.


**IR experience required.** We need fresh networks *and* people who know how to generate leads, pitch, close, and nurture investor relationships post\-close to keep investors warm for the next round. Scripts and support provided. You generate activity; we don't feed leads.


### **About Us**


We're a credible, **funded**, remote\-first startup led by a serial \[technical] founder, and backed by a 20\-person team. The product is live in private alpha.


**Why funded and still raising?** We've raised seed capital. Enough to know the process and pitch works. Now we're raising to scale — ship the product, build the team, and execute. That's why we're building our investor relations team.


Learn more about our founder, team, and comp structures at **list\-lab.org**.


### **Compensation**


Pay\-per\-lead **OR** up to **$300,000** max total compensation in Tier 1 cities; cash and equity components to be negotiated (amount reflects combined cash and equity components).


### **What Success Looks Like in 30 Days**


* You've reached out to your network — emails, DMs, calls — asking for investor referrals
* You've booked calls with qualified prospects or secured warm introductions
* You've asked for referrals on every conversation — no exceptions
* You've established a sustainable rhythm of network outreach


### **What You'll Do**


* **Work your network** — LinkedIn, email, phone. Reach out to people you know and ask for referrals to accredited investors
* **Book calls** — schedule qualified prospects for conversations with our team (or with you, if you're closing)
* **Ask for referrals** — every conversation ends with a request for introductions
* **Qualify leads** — confirm accredited investor status and interest level
* **Close** — work dealflow end\-to\-end, we provide scripts and support for both sourcing and closing
* **Nurture** — keep investors excited with progress updates and milestone achievements along the way


### **What We Provide**


* **Starter scripts** for both sourcing (referral asks, outreach) and closing (pitch, follow\-up)
* **Access to people who have done it** — learn from those already running this playbook
* CRM access, systems, and support


### **What We're Looking For**


* **Existing professional network** that includes or can reach US Accredited Investors (executives, doctors, HNW professionals, etc.)
* **Comfortable asking for referrals** — you don't shy away from "who do you know who might be interested?"
* **Self\-starter** — you generate your own activity from relationships, not from lists we provide
* **Strong communicator** — clear, professional, persuasive
* **IR experience required** — we're looking for people who can work their network *and* have prior investor relations experience – fundraising, LP/GP communication, managed data rooms and referral pipelines


**Nice to have:** B2B sales, account executive, staffing recruiter, or any role where you've built professional relationships and leveraged them.

title: AI Enablement & Strategy Consultant
description:
**WHO WE ARE:** MagicSchool is the premier generative AI platform for teachers. We're just over 2 years old, and more than 7 million teachers from all over the world have joined our platform. Join a top team at a fast growing company that is working towards real social impact. Make an account and try us out at our website and connect with our passionate community on our Wall of Love.

**AI Enablement \& Strategy Consultant**
----------------------------------------

You will serve as a high\-impact architect of MagicSchool's internal AI transformation by consolidating fragmented adoption into a scalable, measurable enablement program. Reporting to Sarah McDaniel, Senior Director of Business Operations, you will build the playbooks and infrastructure necessary for our teams to achieve long\-term AI self\-sufficiency.

**Engagement Details**

* Fixed\-term contract: 4 months
* Estimated commitment: 15 to 20 hours per week
* This role is remote\-flexible; however, some travel to Denver will be expected to support in\-person collaboration with the team

### **Responsibilities**

**In this role, you will be responsible for driving towards the following outcomes:**

* **Functional Enablement \& Playbook Development:** Conduct team by team workflow audits to identify high ROI automation opportunities, resulting in localized AI playbooks and reusable templates for 100% of GTM and Operations functions.
* **Competency Framework \& Integration:** Define clear AI competency expectations for technical and non\-technical roles, providing a structured rubric that leadership can integrate into existing performance and leveling frameworks.
* **Measurement \& Impact Tracking:** Establish a centralized dashboard and methodology to track AI adoption rates and workflow efficiency gains, providing a data informed view of ROI across the organization.
* **Strategic Roadmap \& Tooling Advisory:** Review the current tool stack and governance framework to provide a prioritized 6 to 12 month roadmap, including a formal recommendation on future internal staffing needs for AI enablement.
* **Knowledge Transfer \& Culture Building:** Host regular office hours and functional deep dives to demystify AI concepts for non technical staff, ensuring all engagement artifacts are documented for a seamless handoff.

### **Experience \& Qualifications**

**To be successful in this role, you’ll bring the following experience and qualifications:**

* **Consulting \& Transformation Leadership:** You possess at least 7 years of experience in management consulting with a minimum of 2 years focused specifically on enterprise AI strategy or operational AI transformation.
* **B2B SaaS Expertise:** You have a proven track record of designing enablement programs within technology companies, specifically navigating the unique scaling challenges of Series B through D organizations.
* **Technical Breadth with Practical Application:** You maintain deep familiarity with the current LLM landscape including Claude and ChatGPT Enterprise, along with experience deploying workflow automation tools in non technical environments.
* **Change Management Mastery:** You can translate complex technical concepts into executive ready artifacts and have experience building governance frameworks that accelerate adoption rather than create bureaucracy.
* **Autonomous Operating Style:** You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and can define your own path toward a clear mandate while working approximately 20 hours per week.
* **Mission Alignment:** Ideally, you bring experience within EdTech or mission driven SaaS environments and have a background in organizational development.
* **Consulting Fluency:** You have prior experience in fractional or fixed term engagements and understand how to scope, deliver, and hand off high quality work within a 4 month window.

**Why Join Us?**

* Work on cutting\-edge AI technology that directly impacts educators and students.
* Join a mission\-driven team passionate about making education more efficient and equitable.
* Flexibility of working from home, while fostering a unique culture built on relationships, trust, communication, and collaboration with our team \- no matter where they live.

**For full time employees:**

* Unlimited time off to empower our employees to manage their work\-life balance. We work hard for our teachers and users, and encourage our employees to rest and take the time they need.
* Choice of employer\-paid health insurance plans so that you can take care of yourself and your family. Dental and vision are also offered at very low premiums.
* Every employee is offered generous stock options, vested over 4 years.
* 401k match \& monthly wellness stipend.

**Our Values:**

* **Educators are Magic:** Educators are the most important ingredient in the educational process \- they are the magic, not the AI. Trust them, empower them, and put them at the center of leading change in service of students and families.
* **Joy and Magic:** Bring joy and magic into every learning experience \- push the boundaries of what’s possible with AI.
* **Community:** Foster community that supports one another during a time of rapid technological change. Listen to them and serve their needs.
* **Innovation:** The education system is outdated and in need of innovation and change \- AI is an opportunity to bring equity, access, and serve the individual needs of students better than we ever have before.
* **Responsibility:** Put responsibility and safety at the forefront of the technological change that AI is bringing to education.
* **Diversity:** Diversity of thought, perspectives, and backgrounds helps us serve the wide audience of educators and students around the world.
* **Excellence:** Educators and students deserve the best \- and we strive for the highest quality in everything we do.

title: Senior Staff Accountant
description:
The opportunity

We are an established fashion brand operating across wholesale, e\-commerce, and retail. We are looking for a hands\-on accountant who wants to own the day\-to\-day financial operations of a real business — not manage a team or attend meetings all day, but actually do the work.

This role is ideal for someone with 2–4 years of accounting experience who is ready to step into a senior\-level scope. You will work directly alongside our CFO and have a direct line to the ownership team. The books are yours to run.

A note on the environment: Our brand is a cash\-intensive, operationally complex business. You will encounter real problems that require judgment, not just data entry. If you want a comfortable, low\-stakes role, this is not it. If you want to build something and be recognized for it, read on.

What you will own

**Day\-to\-day accounting operations:**

* Accounts payable: enter bills, code transactions, maintain vendor records, coordinate payment timing with the CFO
* Accounts receivable: apply payments, monitor aging, flag collection issues
* Credit card transactions: code and reconcile all company cards weekly
* Petty cash and cash\-only contractor payments: log, reconcile, and maintain documentation
* Contractor timesheets: collect, verify, and process for payment

**Month\-end close (you own this entirely):**

* Bank reconciliations for all accounts
* Journal entries, accruals, and prepaids
* P\&L and balance sheet review with variance notes for the CFO
* Inventory reconciliation between warehouse and GL
* Clean, on\-time close — target within 10 business days of month end

**Compliance and reporting:**

* Sales tax reconciliation (Shopify/AIMS360 vs. GL/Avalara)
* Payroll processing, coordination and review
* Support the CFO on cash flow forecasting, vendor payment planning, and ad hoc reporting

What we are looking for

* 2–5 years of full\-cycle accounting experience; fashion, apparel, or product\-based company experience is a strong plus
* Solid foundation in QuickBooks Online — you know your way around without hand\-holding
* Comfortable with e\-commerce data flows (Shopify is a plus)
* High attention to detail — you notice when something does not reconcile and you investigate before moving on
* Proactive communicator — you flag issues early, not after they become problems
* Comfortable in a lean, fast\-moving environment where priorities can shift
* Associates or bachelor's degree in accounting or finance; CPA track is a plus but not required

What this role is not

We want to be direct so you can self\-select honestly:

* This is not a supervisory role — there is no team beneath you
* This is not a 9\-to\-5 during close week — month\-end requires focus and flexibility
* This is not a stepping stone to CFO in year one — the CFO role is held by an experienced fractional executive; your path is to become the most trusted operator in the building
* This is not a company with perfect systems — part of what makes this role valuable is that you will help build them

How to stand out

In your application or initial conversation, tell us:

* The most complex reconciliation you have personally worked through — what made it hard and how you solved it
* How you manage your own workload during a month\-end close when unexpected issues come up
* What accounting software and tools you use day to day and how proficient you are in each

Pay: $80,000\.00 \- $90,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Employee discount
* Health insurance
* On\-the\-job training
* Paid sick time
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Sales Account Executive
description:
**About Blue Margin**



Blue Margin helps mid\-market companies become data\-driven. We're a 15\-year\-old Colorado\-based company, and we're growing. We build data lakehouses and dashboard reporting solutions on Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, and Power BI that improve decision\-making and drive measurable business outcomes. Increasingly, that work includes preparing client data for use with modern AI tools, making it easy for their AI applications to connect directly to their data lakehouse for analysis and reporting. Our model is a subscription managed data service, meaning we work closely with clients over the long term as a fractional data team embedded in their business.



We're hiring our fourth Account Executive to help us capitalize on strong momentum at the intersection of data analytics and AI.


**The Role**



You'll own the full sales cycle: finding prospects, building relationships, running discovery, and closing. Once you have a deal underway, you're not on your own. We use a team\-based selling approach, pulling in technical and executive resources at the right moments to support demos, solution design, and closing conversations. Your job is to build the pipeline, run the process, and quarterback the deal. We'll bring the right firepower when it counts to get the deal closed.



We have a real marketing engine behind you: a content\-rich website that draws thousands of monthly visitors, a podcast with over 15,000 subscribers that regularly surfaces deals, AI\-assisted outbound through Clay\-created targeted lists and automated, trigger\-based email sequences, and an outsourced SDR function generating meetings. Inbound and sourced leads flow to the team on a regular basis. That said, this is a self\-sourcing AE role. We want someone who is genuinely comfortable with that and can hit the ground running. If cold\-calling and networking don't sound fun to you, this is probably not the right role.



We're a company that uses AI tools seriously and expects our sales team to do the same. We provide licenses to all major frontier LLMs and use AI\-assisted tools throughout the sales process including Clay for lead generation, HubSpot for CRM, and Gong for call intelligence. If you're already working this way, you'll hit the ground running. If not, we'll get you there.

  


**What You'll Do**


* Build and manage your own pipeline through outbound prospecting, networking, cold outreach, email sequences, and partner relationships
* Sell into mid\-market companies ($100M \- $750M revenue) and PE\-backed portfolio companies looking to improve how they use data and AI
* Own the full sales cycle while leveraging a team\-based approach, bringing in technical and executive resources at the right stages to advance and close opportunities
* Develop fluency in Blue Margin's solutions and the broader Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, Power BI, and AI landscape to credibly engage both business and technical buyers
* Leverage AI\-assisted tools for prospecting, outreach, pipeline management, and call review
* Maintain accurate pipeline data and activity in HubSpot
* Collaborate with delivery and customer success teams to ensure deals are set up for long\-term client success

  


**What We're Looking For**


* 3\+ years of B2B sales experience in technology professional services; candidates with only SaaS sales backgrounds should be aware this is a more consultative, solutions\-oriented sale
* Demonstrated track record of self\-sourcing pipeline, not just closing inbound leads
* Comfortable running your own outbound motion including cold outreach, networking, and multi\-touch email campaigns
* Fluent with modern sales tools and AI\-assisted workflows, or genuinely eager to become so
* Collaborative by nature and effective at orchestrating internal resources to move deals forward
* Consultative selling style with strong discovery and listening skills
* Organized, self\-directed, and accountable to pipeline metrics and revenue targets
* Denver\-based preferred; our LoDo office is steps from Union Station. Some travel to our Fort Collins headquarters expected

  


**What We Offer**


* OTE of $180,000 (base \+ commission), with uncapped upside for overperformance
* Licenses to all major frontier LLMs and a full modern AI toolset for your work
* Opportunity to grow with an established, expanding company in a high\-demand market
* Collaborative team of four AEs with strong internal support at every stage of the deal
* Hybrid work flexibility
* Health, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance
* 401(k) with 4% company match
* PTO, sick leave, and paid holidays
company_description: At Blue Margin, our passion is building a better future for our employees, clients, and community.

* Support budget development, expense tracking, and financial coordination
* Process invoices, timesheets, and administrative records using QuickBooks
* Prepare, track, and manage contracts and renewals
* Maintain compliance records and operational documentation
* Provide day\-to\-day operational support to the President \& CEO
* Coordinate internally with marketing, sales and community engagement efforts as needed
* Identify gaps in processes and implement improvements

**Scope of Role**

This role is focused on internal operations and does not serve as the primary lead for:

* Sales
* Marketing or social media
* Event planning
* HR or IT

**What We’re Looking For**

* Minimum of 5 years experience in operations, administration, or executive support
* Strong systems thinker with attention to detail
* Advanced Microsoft 365 skills (Excel, Teams, SharePoint preferred)
* Experience with QuickBooks or similar financial systems
* Experience working with a CRM system
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines
* Highly dependable with strong organizational habits
* High level of integrity and professionalism
* Naturally organized and process\-driven
* Proactive and solution\-oriented
* College degree preferred
* Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation (mileage reimbursed)
* Ability to pass a background check and drug screening

**Success in This Role Looks Like**

* Systems and processes are clearly documented and easy to follow
* Files and records are organized, current, and accessible
* Deadlines, contracts, and compliance items are consistently tracked and met
* Leadership operates more efficiently because of your structure and support

**Work Environment**

* Primarily office\-based, in\-person role
* Ability to sit, stand, and work at a computer for extended periods
* Occasional lifting of materials (up to 20 lbs)

**How to Apply**

Please submit the following materials via email to klsummerville@visitalbanyga.com:

* Cover letter
* Resume
* List of three professional references

Subject Line: Business Operations Manager Application – \[Your Name]

Please include all materials in one email. Incomplete submissions will not be considered.

In your cover letter, briefly describe a system or process you created or improved.

Pay: $40,000\.00 \- $45,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Experience:

* operations, administration, or executive support: 5 years (Required)

Work Location: In person

Program Development

Training \& Culture

Qualifications

Compensation \& Structure

· Competitive hourly consulting. flexible fractional engagement, with additional material development and referral compensation, as well.

title: Director of Sales
description:
**About Ocra**
--------------

### **Ocra** **is an AI\-powered Revenue Management System (RMS) and Global Distribution System (GDS) built for parking assets. Parking owners and operators use Ocra to sell underutilized parking inventory through “parking OTAs” to prepaid reservation customers looking to park near airports, event venues and high\-demand transient locations.**
**Ocra's team of parking revenue management experts drives revenue growth without adding labor or disrupting operations, helping assets increase net operating income (NOI).**
**Ocra partners with 50\+ parking operators and 25\+ hotel groups at over 600\+ locations. The company has raised $10M, most recently an oversubscribed $5M round co\-led by Trestle Partners and MCR Hotels.**

**About the Role**
------------------


We’re looking for a Director of Sales who loves to build; someone energized by creating structure, unlocking growth, and bringing simplicity to B2B complexity. Ocra is at a true inflection point, with strong and defensible product\-market\-fit, a team of driven AEs already in motion, and strong growing traction across both regional and partnership\-driven sales.  

This role will inherit a series of AI\-Powered SalesOps agents purpose built at Ocra. This is the engine that is powering our next phase of growth. You’ll step into an actively selling team and have the opportunity to work closely with the CEO and other executives to design systems that scale, define clear rules of engagement, and align our two core go\-to\-market motions (a regional, bottoms\-up AE approach and a top\-down partnerships and ownership strategy) so they work seamlessly together.

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Build and run a high\-performing sales cadence (pipeline reviews, 1:1s, QBRs, forecasting)
* Own and optimize HubSpot \- ensuring clean data, clear stages, and accurate ARR reporting
* Develop a forecasting framework that leadership can rely on for decision\-making
* Create scalable systems including playbooks, SOPs, and onboarding materials
* Lead and coach regional AEs across multiple territories, elevating performance and execution
* Provide hands\-on deal support across discovery, strategy, objection handling, and closing
* Set clear performance expectations and drive accountability through data
* Partner cross\-functionally to turn closed deals into expanded, long\-term revenue
* Align and streamline collaboration between Sales and Partnerships, ensuring efficient lead flow and execution
* Partner with the CEO on scaling the sales function, bringing market insights, and hiring to support growth

**What We're Looking For**
--------------------------


We're not looking for a head of quota\-carrying reps or a VP who needs a large team to be effective. We're looking for someone who gets excited about building the machine, not just running it.

* 5–10 years of experience in B2B SaaS sales, including 2–3\+ years in a player\-coach or leadership role
* Proven track record of building and scaling sales infrastructure (playbooks, cadences, CRM, forecasting) in early\- or growth\-stage environments
* Deep HubSpot expertise with a strong understanding of pipeline hygiene, reporting, and data integrity
* Experience managing teams across multiple go\-to\-market motions (e.g., direct \+ partnerships, SMB \+ enterprise)
* Hands\-on leader who coaches reps through deal strategy, skill development, and consistent execution
* Data\-driven operator who uses metrics to guide decisions, performance, and accountability
* Proactive problem\-solver who identifies gaps early and brings thoughtful solutions
* Comfortable in fast\-paced, ambiguous environments. Able to build structure from the ground up
* Strong cross\-functional collaborator, especially with partnerships and leadership teams
* Builder mindset with a focus on creating scalable systems that drive long\-term growth

**Bonus Points**
----------------

* Background in hospitality tech, proptech, or selling to hotel operators or ownership groups
* Experience managing an overlay or partnerships motion alongside a direct AE team
* Familiarity with revenue ops concepts; you don't need to be a RevOps engineer, but you know what good looks like
* You've done a consult\-to\-hire or fractional engagement before and know how to get up to speed fast

**Benefits**
------------

* Competitive salary w/ ancillary earning opportunities
* Comprehensive benefits: medical, dental, \& vision
* Flexible work\-from\-anywhere policy
* Unlimited PTO that supports work\-life balance
* Stock option incentive plan
* Company\-sponsored 401k
* Opportunity to work in a dynamic and growing company
* Collaborative and supportive work environment
* Training and ongoing professional development


Compensation Range: $125K \- $150K

title: Nonprofit Virtual Administrative Assistant (NPVA®)/Fundraising Specialist
description:
**Nonprofit Virtual Administrative Assistant (NPVA®)/Fundraising Specialist**  
**(1099 Contractor Position)**

The More Than Giving Company is seeking experienced virtual administrative assistants to join our exclusive, top\-of\-the\-line, Nonprofit Virtual Assistant (NPVA®) Service. Join an elite team of professionals working with multiple high\-performing nonprofits.

Our NPVA’s are proactive, organized, and excellent project managers. Our clients look to our NPVA’s for guidance and direction, as well as the ability to independently manage administrative operations.

We are seeking individuals to perform a variety of administrative tasks for nonprofit organizations, with a focus on fundraising specific projects. All activities will be performed virtually.

**NPVA tasks include, but are not limited to:**

* Maintain the organization’s master calendar.
* Manage and update organization’s contact database.
* Schedule and manage board meetings and committee meetings; record and distribute notes; attend monthly board meetings virtually; Assist in board engagement activities.
* Assist in donor engagement and in drafting solicitation letters; Manage and update donor/sponsor information.
* Provide administrative support to fundraising and special events; Maintain event registration.
* Manage email and process general correspondence. Document management and records retention.
* Light bookkeeping; general project management.

**Fundraising specific tasks include:**

* Manage donor database (CRM), including data entry, gift tracking, and reporting to ensure accurate records and insights
* Process donations and execute timely donor acknowledgments, including thank\-you messages and tax receipts
* Support fundraising campaigns and communications, including email marketing, list segmentation, and performance tracking
* Assist with event and campaign coordination, including logistics, attendee management, and follow\-up
* Provide administrative and research support, including donor prospecting, grant tracking, scheduling, and stewardship activities

**Professional Requirements:**

* Minimum of 2 years as a virtual administrative assistant (no exceptions)
* Minimum of 3 years working with nonprofit organizations (no exceptions)
* Minimum 5 years as an Administrative/Executive Assistant is required (no exceptions)
* College degree is preferred, some college or related certification is required
* Excellent time management, organizational, and written and oral communication skills
* Technical proficiency in MS Office, MailChimp/Constant Contact, Dropbox, Google Suite, Zoom, Trello/Asana, Eventbrite, Calendly, LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram

**Additional:**

* Maintain reliable high\-speed internet connection, computer hardware and software, reliable phone/headset. Hired contractors are required to have their own equipment.
* Be based in the U.S. and agree to a criminal background check.
* Provide 3 references and two writing samples.
* Agree to complete The More than Giving Co NPVA® certification program.
* Secure professional liability insurance.

*The More Than Giving Company is an organization committed to helping nonprofit organizations create sustainable growth. Established in 1998, MTG is a nonprofit fractional staffing company providing all levels of staffing to nonprofit organizations looking to grow and thrive. As a company, we are committed to building and maintaining a team of seasoned nonprofit professionals who serve the best interests of our nonprofit clients by delivering best practices, tested solutions, and sustainable advice. To learn more, visit www.morethangiving.co.*

3/16/2026

Job Type: Contract

Pay: $24\.00 per hour

Application Question(s):

* Describe your work experience in the nonprofit sector (Required)
* Describe your virtual assistant experience (Required)
* Are you willing to work as a 1099 contractor?
* Are you based in the US?

Experience:

* nonprofit: 3 years (Required)
* administrative assistant: 5 years (Required)
* virtual: 1 year (Required)

Work Location: Remote
